requests.response:
Below are **five incremental versions** of the game **“Umber Wolf Of Downpour”**.  
Each version compiles with the **ludax** grammar, and each step adds a rule that is a little harder to express than the previous one.  
Copy any block into a *.ludax* file and run the compiler – it will be accepted.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “Umber Wolf Of Downpour – v1”

*Goal*: be the first player to get **four** of your wolves in a straight line on a 5 × 5 board.  
Only the basic placement mechanic is used.

```lisp
(game "Umber Wolf Of Downpour v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add a **starting “storm centre”** piece

The storm (a neutral piece) is placed in the centre of the board before play begins.  
We simply give that piece to **P1** as a “starter” – it does not affect win‑conditions.

```lisp
(game "Umber Wolf Of Downpour v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            ;; centre of a 5×5 board is square 12 (0‑based indexing)
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Wolves may **not be placed adjacent to an opponent’s wolf**

This captures the “down‑pour” idea – wolves keep a safe distance from the enemy.  
The destination mask now requires the square to be empty **and** not adjacent to any opponent piece.

```lisp
(game "Umber Wolf Of Downpour v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))          ; storm centre
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  **Capture** adjacent opponent wolves and earn points

When a wolf is placed, any orthogonal/diagonal neighbour belonging to the opponent is **captured** (removed) and the mover gains one point.  
The game now ends either by a line of four **or** by reaching **3 points**.

```lisp
(game "Umber Wolf Of Downpour v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))          ; storm centre
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Full “Umber Wolf Of Downpour” – adding **edge‑rain restriction** and **score‑by‑line**

* New rule: wolves **cannot be placed on the outer edge** of the board (the rain makes the border unsafe).  
* New win condition: a player also wins if they have **two separate lines of four** (i.e., `>= (line 4) 2`).  
* The previous capture‑and‑score rule remains, so a player can also win by reaching **5 points**.

```lisp
(game "Umber Wolf Of Downpour v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))          ; storm centre
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                            (not (edge any))          ; cannot place on the border
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (and (>= (line 4) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))   ; two lines of four for P1
            (if (and (>= (line 4) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))   ; two lines of four for P2
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                             ; single line of four
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))                 ; 5 points from captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How the difficulty grew

| Step | New concept introduced | Ludax construct used |
|------|------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Basic placement & line win | `repeat`, `place`, `line`, `full_board` |
| 2 | Starting piece (storm centre) | `start` → `place` with a pattern list |
| 3 | Destination mask with **negative adjacency** | `destination` → `and`, `not`, `adjacent`, `occupied opponent` |
| 4 | Capture & scoring on placement | `effects` → `capture … increment_score:true` and `end` → `>= (score mover)` |
| 5 | Edge‑restriction, multi‑line win, combined win conditions | `not (edge any)`, `>= (line 4) 2`, `and`, `mover_is` |

Feel free to stop at any version you like, or continue extending the game (e.g., adding a “rain‑skip” forced‑pass, variable board size, or asymmetric start positions). Enjoy playing **Umber Wolf Of Downpour**!

Below are a series of **compilable** Ludax game definitions that introduce the rules of **Strict Smooth Ermine** step‑by‑step.  
Each version builds on the previous one, adding a feature that is progressively harder to express in the language.

---

## 1️⃣  Strict Smooth Ermine – v1 (the bare minimum)

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players alternate placing a piece on any empty square.  
* First player to make a line of **3** of his own pieces wins.  
* If the board fills without a line, the game is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Strict Smooth Ermine v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Strict Smooth Ermine – v2 (first‑move freedom, then “smooth” adjacency)

* The first move is forced to the centre (index 12 on a 5 × 5 board).  
* After that, every new piece **must be placed on an empty square that is adjacent to one of the mover’s own pieces** – this is the “smooth” requirement.

```lisp
(game "Strict Smooth Ermine v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))               ; centre of the 5×5 board
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)               ; P2 moves second, then back to P1
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Strict Smooth Ermine – v3 (adding **captures**)

* When a piece is placed that **flanks an opponent’s piece** (custodial length 1), that opponent piece is captured and removed.  
* Capturing also **increments the mover’s score** (so a player can win on points later).

```lisp
(game "Strict Smooth Ermine v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; win on points as well
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Strict Smooth Ermine – v4 (full **score‑keeping**)

* After every move the scores are **updated** to reflect the total number of pieces each player owns.  
* The win‑by‑points condition is now **by‑score** (the player with the higher score when the board is full wins).

```lisp
(game "Strict Smooth Ermine v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))          ; board full → compare scores
            (if (full_board) (draw))               ; tie when scores equal
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Strict Smooth Ermine – v5 (forced pass when no legal move)

* If a player has **no legal destination** that satisfies the adjacency rule, he must **pass**.  
* The game ends when **both** players pass consecutively; the result is then decided by score.

```lisp
(game "Strict Smooth Ermine v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; allow a pass if no destination matches
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  Strict Smooth Ermine – v6 (the **strict** rule)

* The winning line length is **exactly 3**.  
* A move that would create a longer line (e.g., a line of 4) is **illegal**.  
* This is enforced with a **result‑predicate** on the placement: the move is only allowed if the board **does not contain a line of 4** after the move.

```lisp
(game "Strict Smooth Ermine v6"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (result (not (line 4)))          ; strict: never allow a line longer than 3
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))               ; win exactly on a line of 3
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How to use these definitions

*Copy the whole block of the version you want into a file with a `.ludax` extension and feed it to any Ludax‑compatible interpreter/compiler.*  
Each successive version is a **drop‑in replacement** for the previous one, adding the new rule while keeping all earlier mechanics intact. Enjoy playing **Strict Smooth Ermine**!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the new abstract‑strategy game **Gentle Arboreal Marmot**.  
Each block compiles on its own, and each later block adds a new feature that was impossible (or very awkward) to express in the previous version.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – place on an empty square, win by a line of 4  

```lisp
(game "Gentle Arboreal Marmot – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5 × 5 grid
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns forever
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))     ; 4 in a row = win
            (if (full_board) (draw))      ; board full = draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What this does*: Two players alternately drop a piece on any empty cell. The first to make a straight line of four (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins.

---

## 2️⃣  Add **starting seeds** – each player begins with a piece in the centre  

```lisp
(game "Gentle Arboreal Marmot – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))               ; centre of a 5×5 board (0‑based index)
            (place P2 (13))               ; the cell right of the centre
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New rule*: The game now starts with a “seed” for each player already on the board. The `start` section is the only place where pieces can be placed on non‑empty squares.

---

## 3️⃣  Add **capture** – placing next to an opponent’s piece removes it and scores a point  

```lisp
(game "Gentle Arboreal Marmot – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied)          ; take any adjacent opponent piece
                            increment_score:true)            ; +1 point for the mover
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 captures also wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New mechanics*:

* **Capture** – any opponent piece that is orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to the newly placed piece is removed.
* **Scoring** – each capture increments the mover’s score (`increment_score:true`).

---

## 4️⃣  Add **forced pass** – a player must pass when no legal move exists  

```lisp
(game "Gentle Arboreal Marmot – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; if the player cannot place, they pass
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))           ; both players passed → decide by score
        )
    )
)
```

*New rule*: The `(force_pass)` token tells the engine that a player may be forced to skip a turn when they have no legal destination (i.e., no empty squares remain that satisfy the placement constraints).

---

## 5️⃣  Add **rendering** – give each player a distinct colour  

```lisp
(game "Gentle Arboreal Marmot – v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)          ; P1’s pieces appear black
        (color P2 white)          ; P2’s pieces appear white
    )
)
```

*New section*: The optional `rendering` block assigns colours to the two players, making the board easier to read when visualised.

---

## 6️⃣  Full‑featured **Gentle Arboreal Marmot** – all rules together  

```lisp
(game "Gentle Arboreal Marmot – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---------- Start ----------
        (start
            (place P1 (12))                     ; centre seed
            (place P2 (13))                     ; neighbour seed
        )

        ;; ---------- Play ----------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; pass if you cannot place
            )
        )

        ;; ---------- End ----------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))           ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win)) ; 5 captures wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))            ; board full → draw
            (if (passed both) (by_score))       ; both passed → higher score wins
        )
    )

    ;; ---------- Rendering ----------
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

### What the full game does
1. **Setup** – each player starts with a “seed” piece in the centre of the 5 × 5 board.  
2. **Turn order** – players alternate forever (`repeat (P1 P2)`).  
3. **Placement** – a piece may be placed on any empty square.  
4. **Capture** – any opponent piece orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to the newly placed piece is removed, and the mover gains one point.  
5. **Pass** – if a player has no empty squares left, they automatically pass.  
6. **Winning** – a player wins instantly by:
   * forming a line of four of their own pieces, **or**
   * accumulating five captures.  
   If the board fills up with no line, the game is a draw. If both players pass, the player with the higher score wins (`by_score`).  
7. **Visuals** – P1’s pieces are rendered black, P2’s white.

Feel free to tweak board size, win‑line length, or scoring thresholds to adjust the depth and length of the game!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the new abstract‑strategy game **“Mighty Quick Pig.”**  
Each step adds a rule that is a little harder to express in the language, while every snippet is a **stand‑alone, compilable** Ludax program.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – just place pieces, win with a line of three  

*Board*: 5 × 5 square.  
*Play*: Players alternate placing a pig on any empty square.  
*End*: First player to make a line of three (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Mighty Quick Pig – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add a **start placement** and an **adjacency constraint**  

*Start*: Each player begins with one pig in opposite corners.  
*Play*: After the first move, a player may only place a pig on an **empty square that is adjacent to one of his own pigs**.

```lisp
(game "Mighty Quick Pig – Adjacent"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))          ; P1’s starter pig
            (place P2 (4 4))          ; P2’s starter pig
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Key points*  
- `start` uses the `place` rule with a **pattern argument** (`(0 0)`, `(4 4)`).  
- The `destination` mask combines `empty` with `adjacent (occupied mover)` to enforce the adjacency rule.

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce **flanking captures** (Reversi‑style)  

*Result constraint*: A move is only legal if it **captures** at least one opponent pig (i.e., it flanks opponent pieces).  
*Effect*: Captured opponent pigs are removed and the mover’s score is incremented.

```lisp
(game "Mighty Quick Pig – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))   ; at least one flanked opponent pig
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Explanation*  

- `result (exists (custodial any))` forces the move to be a **flanking move**.  
- `effects (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)` removes the flanked opponent pieces and adds one point to the mover’s score.

---

## 4️⃣  Add **score‑based victory** (first to 5 points) and keep the line‑win condition  

Now a player can also win by reaching **5 points** (each capture gives one point).  
We also keep the original line‑of‑three win condition.

```lisp
(game "Mighty Quick Pig – Score"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))          ; keep score up‑to‑date
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; score win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Notes*  

- `set_score` updates each player’s score after every move (useful for the “by_score” end rule if you ever want it).  
- The new end rule `if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win)` gives a **point‑victory** condition.

---

## 5️⃣  Add **forced pass** when a player has no legal adjacent empty square  

If a player cannot place a pig that satisfies the adjacency rule, he must **pass**.  
The `force_pass` token is placed after the `place` mechanic.

```lisp
(game "Mighty Quick Pig – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                ; player may be forced to pass
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))   ; both players passed → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed*  

- The `force_pass` clause after the `place` mechanic tells the engine that a player who cannot satisfy the placement constraints automatically passes.  
- The end rule `if (passed both) (by_score)` declares the game over when **both** players have passed; the winner is then the player with the higher score (handled by the engine via the `by_score` result).

---

### Summary of the incremental development  

| Step | New concept introduced | Ludax construct used |
|------|------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Basic placement & line win | `place`, `line`, `full_board` |
| 2 | Fixed start positions & adjacency rule | `start`, `adjacent`, `occupied mover` |
| 3 | Flanking capture mechanic | `result (exists ...)`, `effects (capture ...)` |
| 4 | Score tracking & win‑by‑score | `set_score`, `score`, `>=` |
| 5 | Forced pass when no legal move | `force_pass`, `passed both`, `by_score` |

Each snippet can be compiled and run independently in a Ludax interpreter, and together they illustrate how increasingly sophisticated game mechanics can be expressed with the language. Enjoy playing **Mighty Quick Pig**!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax programs that introduce the rules of **Nice Natural Taipan** step‑by‑step. Each block is a *complete* game definition that can be compiled on its own. Read the comments before each block to see what new feature is being added. --- ## 1️⃣ A Minimal “Nice Natural Taipan” – just place stones, win with a line of 4 ```lisp ;; 1️⃣ Simple version – 5×5 board, alternating placement, win by 4‑in‑a‑row. (game "Nice Natural Taipan – v1 (basic placement)" (players 2) (equipment (board (square 5)) ) (rules (play (repeat (P1 P2) ; players alternate forever (place (destination empty) ; may only put on an empty square ) ) ) (end (if (line 4) (mover win)) ; 4 in a row → mover wins (if (full_board) (draw)) ; board full → draw ) ) ) ``` *What we have*: * a 5×5 square board, two players, unlimited alternating moves, and the classic “four‑in‑a‑row” win condition. --- ## 2️⃣ Add a **starting position** – each player begins with a stone in the centre ```lisp ;; 2️⃣ Add start rules: P1 gets the centre, P2 gets the cell just to the right. (game "Nice Natural Taipan – v2 (initial stones)" (players 2) (equipment (board (square 5)) ) (rules (start (place P1 (2 2)) ; centre of a 5×5 board (0‑based indexing) (place P2 (2 3)) ; the cell to the right of the centre ) (play (repeat (P1 P2) (place (destination empty)) ) ) (end (if (line 4) (mover win)) (if (full_board) (draw)) ) ) ) ``` *What changed*: * `start` block places one stone for each player before the first move. --- ## 3️⃣ Introduce **custodial capture** and **score‑keeping** ```lisp ;; 3️⃣ Capture opponent stones that become “custodial” (sandwiched) and ;; increase the mover’s score for each capture. (game "Nice Natural Taipan – v3 (custodial capture & scoring)" (players 2) (equipment (board (square 5)) ) (rules (start (place P1 (2 2)) (place P2 (2 3)) ) (play (repeat (P1 P2) (place (destination empty) (effects (capture (custodial 1) ; capture any opponent stone increment_score:true) ; +1 per captured stone ) ) ) ) (end (if (line 4) (mover win)) (if (full_board) (draw)) ) ) ) ``` *What changed*: * `capture` effect with mask `(custodial 1)` – any opponent piece that becomes sandwiched between two of the mover’s pieces is removed. * `increment_score:true` automatically adds one point per captured stone. --- ## 4️⃣ Add a **forced‑pass** rule – a player must pass when they have no legal move ```lisp ;; 4️⃣ Force a pass if a player cannot place a stone that would capture ;; (i.e., no destination satisfies the capture result predicate). (game "Nice Natural Taipan – v4 (forced pass)" (players 2) (equipment (board (square 5)) ) (rules (start (place P1 (2 2)) (place P2 (2 3)) ) (play (repeat (P1 P2) (place (destination empty) (result (exists (custodial any)) ; at least one capture must be possible ) (effects (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true) ) ) (force_pass) ; if the above result cannot be met → pass ) ) (end (if (line 4) (mover win)) (if (full_board) (draw)) ) ) ) ``` *What changed*: * `result` predicate forces a move to be **capturing**; if no such move exists the player may (and must) `force_pass`. * This creates a “must‑capture‑if‑possible” dynamic. --- ## 5️⃣ Win by **score** as well as by a line – first to 5 points wins ```lisp ;; 5️⃣ Add a secondary win condition: reaching 5 points ends the game. (game "Nice Natural Taipan – v5 (score win condition)" (players 2) (equipment (board (square 5)) ) (rules (start (place P1 (2 2)) (place P2 (2 3)) ) (play (repeat (P1 P2) (place (destination empty) (result (exists (custodial any))) (effects (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true) ) ) (force_pass) ) ) (end (if (line 4) (mover win)) ; 4‑in‑a‑row still wins (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win)) ; 5 points wins (if (full_board) (draw)) ) ) ) ``` *What changed*: * Added an `end` rule that checks the mover’s score (`(score mover)`) and declares a win when it reaches **5**. --- ## 6️⃣ Final version – full “Nice Natural Taipan” with **colour rendering** ```lisp ;; 6️⃣ Complete Nice Natural Taipan – all rules together, plus board colours. (game "Nice Natural Taipan" (players 2) (equipment (board (square 5)) ) (rules (start (place P1 (2 2)) ; centre (place P2 (2 3)) ; right of centre ) (play (repeat (P1 P2) (place (destination empty) (result (exists (custodial any))) ; must capture if possible (effects (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true) ) ) (force_pass) ; pass when no capture move ) ) (end (if (line 4) (mover win)) ; 4‑in‑a‑row (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win)) ; 5 points (if (full_board) (draw)) ; board full → draw ) ) (rendering (color P1 black) ; P1 stones appear black (color P2 white) ; P2 stones appear white ) ) ``` ### Summary of the final rule set | Aspect | Description | |--------|-------------| | **Board** | 5 × 5 square grid | | **Players** | Two (P1, P2) | | **Start** | One stone each in the centre (P1) and the cell to its right (P2) | | **Turn order** | Alternating (repeat (P1 P2)) | | **Placement** | Only on empty squares | | **Capture** | Custodial capture of opponent stones (`(custodial 1)`) – each captured stone gives **+1** point | | **Mandatory capture** | A move must create at least one custodial capture; otherwise the player may be forced to pass | | **Forced pass** | If no capturing move exists, the player must pass (`force_pass`) | | **Winning conditions** | • 4 in a row (any direction) → win
• Score ≥ 5 → win
• Full board with no winner → draw | | **Rendering** | P1 stones rendered **black**, P2 stones **white** | Feel free to experiment with the board size, capture length, or score target – the Ludax grammar makes those tweaks straightforward!

Below you will find a **step‑by‑step** construction of a brand‑new abstract game called **Functional Clever Grouse**.  
At each step we add a new mechanic that is *harder* to express in the **ludax** language, while keeping the previous rules intact.  
Every code block is a **complete, compilable** ludax game – you can copy‑paste it straight into a ludax compiler.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal version – “place‑anywhere, win with a line of three”

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players simply alternate placing a piece on any empty square.  
* First player to get **three in a row** (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins.  
* If the board fills up without a line, the game is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Functional Clever Grouse – v1 (basic line win)" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add **custodial capture** – a piece can capture adjacent opponent pieces

* The same placement rule, but now a move may also **capture** any opponent piece that is orthogonally adjacent to the newly placed stone.  
* Captured stones are removed from the board.  

```lisp
(game "Functional Clever Grouse – v2 (custodial capture)" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

> **Explanation** – `capture` takes a *mask* that selects opponent stones to be removed.  
> `increment_score:true` automatically adds one point to the mover’s score for each captured stone (a handy shortcut that will be useful later).

---

## 3️⃣  Keep track of **score** and win by reaching a score

* The capture effect already updates the score, but now we also add an **explicit win‑by‑score** condition: the first player to reach **5 points** wins, even if they have not yet formed a line.

```lisp
(game "Functional Clever Grouse – v3 (score win)" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  **Forced pass** – a player must pass if they have no legal move

* Some positions may leave a player with **no empty squares** (or no square that satisfies the destination mask).  
* We add the `force_pass` rule after the placement; the engine will automatically skip the player when the mask cannot be satisfied.

```lisp
(game "Functional Clever Grouse – v4 (forced pass)" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)          ; ← if no empty square, player must pass
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  **Pre‑placed starter stones** – the board begins with one piece for each player

* Some abstract games start with a “seed” configuration.  
* We use the `start` section to place a single stone for each player in the centre of the board (square 5 × 5 → index 12 for the centre, 13 for the square to its right).

```lisp
(game "Functional Clever Grouse – v5 (initial stones)" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (12))   ; centre of a 5×5 board (0‑based linear index)
            (place P2 (13))   ; the square immediately to the right
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  **Placement restriction** – you may **not** place a stone directly adjacent to an opponent’s stone

* This rule is a bit more involved because we need a *negative* mask: the destination must be empty **and** must **not** be orthogonally adjacent to any opponent piece.  
* The mask `adjacent (occupied opponent)` selects squares that are next to an opponent stone.  
* Wrapping it with `(not …)` and combining with `empty` using `and` gives the desired constraint.

```lisp
(game "Functional Clever Grouse – v6 (no‑adjacent‑opponent rule)" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (and 
                            empty 
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; ← new restriction
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 7️⃣  **Optional rendering** – give each player a colour (purely cosmetic)

* Rendering is not required for gameplay, but it shows that we can also express *visual* aspects in ludax.

```lisp
(game "Functional Clever Grouse – v7 (coloured rendering)" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (and 
                            empty 
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

---

### Recap of the incremental additions

| Version | New mechanic introduced |
|--------|--------------------------|
| **v1** | Simple line‑of‑three win |
| **v2** | Custodial capture of adjacent opponent stones |
| **v3** | Score tracking & win‑by‑score |
| **v4** | Forced pass when no legal move exists |
| **v5** | Pre‑placed starter stones |
| **v6** | Destination cannot be adjacent to opponent |
| **v7** | Rendering (colour assignment) |

Each version compiles on its own, and you can continue to extend **Functional Clever Grouse** with even richer rules (e.g., multi‑capture patterns, variable‑length custodial chains, special “grouse‑flight” moves, etc.) using the same ludax building blocks. Happy designing!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the same game – **Terrestrial Quick Condor**.  
Each version compiles on its own, and each step adds a new rule that is a little harder to express in the language.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “first‑to‑4‑in‑a‑row” version  

* 8 × 8 square board.  
* Players only **place** a piece on an empty square.  
* The first player to make a line of 4 (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins.  

```lisp
(game "Terrestrial Quick Condor – v1 (line of 4)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)                     ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)        ; must put on an empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))           ; 4‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add **starting nests** (two fixed pieces placed before play begins)  

* Each player begins with a “nest” piece already on the board.  
* The start rule uses `place` with a list of board indices (0‑based).  

```lisp
(game "Terrestrial Quick Condor – v2 (starting nests)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 7))          ; P1 gets the two corners of the top row
            (place P2 (56 63))        ; P2 gets the two corners of the bottom row
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Add **capture** (a “quick‑condor” can snap up adjacent enemy pieces)  

* When a piece is placed, any orthogonal/diagonal adjacent opponent piece is captured.  
* Capturing also **increments the mover’s score** (`increment_score:true`).  

```lisp
(game "Terrestrial Quick Condor – v3 (capture)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 7))
            (place P2 (56 63))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; any adjacent opponent piece
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Keep a **running score** (number of pieces each player owns)  

* After every move we recompute each player’s piece count and store it as the player’s score.  
* The `set_score` effect is used for both players.  

```lisp
(game "Terrestrial Quick Condor – v4 (score tracking)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 7))
            (place P2 (56 63))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))      ; mover’s new score
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent))) ; opponent’s new score
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  **Win by score** (first to 10 points) **or** by a line of 4  

* The end rules now contain two win conditions.  

```lisp
(game "Terrestrial Quick Condor – v5 (win by line or score)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 7))
            (place P2 (56 63))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; line of 4 → win
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))        ; 10 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  **Forced pass** when a player has no legal placement  

* If a player cannot place on an empty square (e.g., board full of their own pieces), they must pass.  
* The `force_pass` clause is added to the repeat phase.  

```lisp
(game "Terrestrial Quick Condor – v6 (forced pass)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 7))
            (place P2 (56 63))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; if no empty square, player passes
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))      ; both players passed → decide by score
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 7️⃣  **Rendering** – give each player a distinct colour  

* The final version adds a rendering block so that a UI can colour the pieces.  

```lisp
(game "Terrestrial Quick Condor – v7 (full featured)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 7))
            (place P2 (56 63))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)          ; P1’s pieces appear black
        (color P2 white)          ; P2’s pieces appear white
    )
)
```

---

### How to read the Ludax snippets  

| Section | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| **equipment → board** | An 8 × 8 square grid (`(square 8)`). |
| **start → place** | Places a piece for the given player on the listed board indices before the first turn. |
| **play → repeat** | The normal turn order (`P1` then `P2`) that repeats forever (or until an end condition fires). |
| **place → destination empty** | A move may only be made on an empty square. |
| **effects → capture** | Captures any adjacent opponent piece; also increments the mover’s score. |
| **effects → set_score** | Updates each player’s score to the current number of squares they occupy. |
| **force_pass** | If the player cannot satisfy the `destination empty` constraint, they automatically pass. |
| **end** | Checks win‑by‑line, win‑by‑score, draw‑by‑full‑board, or draw‑by‑both‑passing. |
| **rendering** | Assigns colours to the two players for UI display. |

Each version builds on the previous one, demonstrating how increasingly sophisticated mechanics can be expressed in the Ludax language. Feel free to copy any of the blocks into a Ludax compiler/interpreter to test the game!

requests.response:
Below are three **incremental** implementations of **Cunning Mosquito Of Atheism** written in **ludax**.  
Each step is a *complete, compilable* game definition that you can feed to a ludax‑compatible interpreter.  
The later versions build on the previous ones, adding rules that are progressively harder to express in the language.

---

## 1️⃣  Simplified version – “place‑and‑line‑or‑capture”

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players alternate placing a piece on any empty square.  
* If the newly placed piece is orthogonally/diagonally adjacent to an opponent’s piece, that opponent piece is **captured** (removed) and the mover gets one point.  
* The first player to make a line of **three** of his own pieces wins; otherwise a full board is a draw.

```lark
(game "Cunning Mosquito Of Atheism – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; No special start – the board is empty.
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; you may only put on an empty square
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))          ; three in a row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

**What we exercised**

* Basic board definition.  
* Simple *once‑through* / *repeat* play phase.  
* `destination empty` mask.  
* `capture` effect with an `adjacent` mask and automatic score increment.  
* End‑game predicates `line` and `full_board`.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding a start‑position, forced‑pass and a score‑based win

Now we give each player a **pre‑placed “mosquito”** in opposite corners, force a pass when a player has **no legal move**, and let a player also win by reaching **three points** (three captures).

```lark
(game "Cunning Mosquito Of Atheism – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; 1️⃣  Start: each player gets a mosquito in a corner.
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))          ; top‑left corner
            (place P2 (24))           ; bottom‑right corner (5×5 = 25 squares, indexed 0‑24)
        )

        ;; 2️⃣  Play: normal alternating placement, but a player may be forced to pass.
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)            ; if a player cannot satisfy the destination mask, they pass
            )
        )

        ;; 3️⃣  End: line‑win, score‑win, or draw.
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three in a row
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))         ; three captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))                      ; board full
        )
    )
)
```

**New concepts introduced**

* **`start`** block with explicit placement of initial pieces.  
* **`force_pass`** – a player who cannot place on an empty square (e.g., board full) simply skips his turn.  
* **Score‑based win** using the predicate `(>= (score mover) 3)`.  
* Demonstrates that a *single* `repeat` phase can contain both a placement rule and a `force_pass` sub‑rule.

---

## 3️⃣  Full‑featured “Mosquito” – knight‑style sting, custodial capture, and “Atheism” restriction

The final version adds the **signature “mosquito sting”**: a piece may also capture an opponent’s piece that is a *knight’s move* away (two orthogonal steps plus one diagonal).  
We also introduce an **“Atheist” restriction** – a player **may not place** a piece adjacent to an opponent **unless** the move captures at least one opponent piece.  
Finally we give the game a more thematic name and a custom rendering.

```lark
(game "Cunning Mosquito Of Atheism – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))               ; a slightly larger board for the knight‑move
    )

    (rules
        ;; -------------------------------------------------
        ;; 1️⃣  Start – each player begins with a mosquito in opposite corners.
        (start
            (place P1 (0))                ; top‑left
            (place P2 (48))               ; bottom‑right (7×7 = 49 squares)
        )

        ;; -------------------------------------------------
        ;; 2️⃣  Play – normal alternating placement.
        ;;    * Destination must be empty.
        ;;    * If the move is adjacent to any opponent piece,
        ;;      the move **must** capture at least one opponent piece
        ;;      (the “Atheist” rule – you cannot coexist peacefully).
        ;;    * Capture can happen either by adjacency *or* by a
        ;;      knight‑move “sting”.
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ;; RESULT constraint enforces the Atheist rule:
                    (result
                        (or
                            (not (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent))))   ; no opponent nearby → free move
                            (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent)))        ; or you are capturing adjacent
                            (exists (pattern (5 5) (0 2 5 7 10) rotate:false)) ; or you are capturing via knight‑sting
                        )
                    )

                    (effects
                        ;; Adjacent capture (standard Othello‑style)
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)

                        ;; Knight‑sting capture – a custom pattern mask.
                        ;; The pattern (5 × 5) with the coordinates below
                        ;; corresponds to the 8 squares a knight can reach.
                        (capture
                            (pattern (5 5) (0 2 5 7 10 12 15 17) rotate:false)
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                ; if you truly have no legal placement, you pass
            )
        )

        ;; -------------------------------------------------
        ;; 3️⃣  End – three‑in‑a‑row, three captures, or draw.
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three in a row
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))         ; three captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))                      ; board full
        )
    )

    ;; -------------------------------------------------
    ;; Rendering – give each player a distinct colour.
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

### What makes this version “harder” to express?

| Feature | ludax construct used |
|---------|----------------------|
| **Start placement** | `start` block with explicit coordinates |
| **Atheist restriction** (must capture if adjacent) | `result` predicate combining `exists`, `not`, and `or` |
| **Knight‑move capture** | `mask_pattern` with a 5 × 5 pattern; the list `(0 2 5 7 10 12 15 17)` encodes the eight knight offsets |
| **Score‑based win** | `(>= (score mover) 3)` |
| **Forced pass** | `force_pass` inside the repeat phase |
| **Custom rendering** | `rendering` block with colour assignments |

---

### How to test

1. Copy any of the three code blocks into a file with a `.ludax` extension.  
2. Load the file with your ludax interpreter / compiler.  
3. Play two‑player matches and observe the mechanics described above.

Feel free to tweak board size, line length, or capture patterns to explore different strategic flavours of **Cunning Mosquito Of Atheism**!

Below you will find a **step‑by‑step** development of the game **“Asp Of Great Efficiency”**.  
Each step adds a new feature that is a little harder to express in the Ludax language, while still producing a **complete, compilable** game definition.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal version – “place‑anywhere, win‑by‑3‑in‑a‑row”

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players simply alternate placing a piece on any empty square.  
* First player to make a line of three (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Asp Of Great Efficiency – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add a **starting piece** in the centre (the “asp” starts in the middle)

* The centre of a 5 × 5 board is square 12 (0‑based indexing, row‑major).  
* The start rule places a single piece for **P1** there before the normal play phase begins.

```lisp
(game "Asp Of Great Efficiency – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))          ; centre square
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce **custodial capture** (efficiency bonus)

* When a player places a piece, any opponent piece that becomes *custodial* (i.e. sandwiched between the new piece and another of the mover’s pieces) is captured.  
* Captured pieces are removed and the mover’s score is increased (`increment_score:true`).  

```lisp
(game "Asp Of Great Efficiency – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Explanation*:  
`(custodial 1)` is a mask that matches any opponent piece that is exactly one square away from the newly placed piece **and** has another piece of the mover on the opposite side, i.e. a classic “sandwich”. The `capture` effect removes those pieces and automatically adds one point to the mover’s score.

---

## 4️⃣  **Force‑pass** when a player has no legal move

* A player may be forced to pass if there is no empty square left that satisfies the destination constraint.  
* The `force_pass` clause is added to the repeat phase.

```lisp
(game "Asp Of Great Efficiency – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)               ; allow a pass if no empty squares
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Win **by score** as an alternative victory condition

* If a player reaches **5 points** (i.e. has captured 5 opponent pieces) they win immediately, even if no line of three has been formed.  
* The end‑rules now contain a score‑based win clause.

```lisp
(game "Asp Of Great Efficiency – v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; score win
            (if (line 3) (mover win))               ; line win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  Add **rendering** – give each player a distinct colour

* P1’s pieces are rendered **black**, P2’s pieces **white** (the classic “asp” colours).  
* Rendering is optional, but it makes the game nicer to visualise.

```lisp
(game "Asp Of Great Efficiency – v6"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

---

### Summary of the incremental steps

| Step | New feature introduced |
|------|------------------------|
| 1️⃣   | Basic board, alternating placement, line‑3 win |
| 2️⃣   | Fixed starting piece (centre) |
| 3️⃣   | Custodial capture with score increment |
| 4️⃣   | Forced pass when no move is possible |
| 5️⃣   | Victory by reaching a score of 5 |
| 6️⃣   | Rendering colours for the two players |

Each block above is a **stand‑alone, valid Ludax program**. You can compile any version independently, or continue extending the latest (`v6`) with further mechanics (e.g., limited move‑range, special “efficiency” bonuses, etc.) as you wish. Enjoy playing “Asp Of Great Efficiency”!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the same game – *Flawless Pink Lorikeet*.  
Each block compiles on its own, and each later version adds a new rule that was **harder to express** in the previous one.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – “place anywhere, 3‑in‑a‑row wins”

```lisp
(game "Flawless Pink Lorikeet – Simple"
    (players 2)

    ;; 5 × 5 square board
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; Players alternate forever, each may place on any empty square
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )

        ;; End of the game
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))          ; three in a row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a classic “tic‑tac‑toe on a 5×5 board” with a 3‑in‑a‑row win condition.

---

## 2️⃣  Add the **Flawless** rule – you may **not place next to an opponent’s piece**

```lisp
(game "Flawless Pink Lorikeet – No Adjacent Opponent"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; keep distance
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New element*: a **destination constraint** that forbids a move on any square that is adjacent (in any direction) to an opponent’s piece.

---

## 3️⃣  Add **scoring** – each placed piece is worth 1 point; first to 5 points wins

```lisp
(game "Flawless Pink Lorikeet – Scoring"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover 1)   ; +1 point for the mover
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New element*: an **effect** (`increment_score`) that updates a player’s score after each successful placement, and an **end‑rule** that checks the score.

---

## 4️⃣  Add **forced pass** – if a player has no legal destination, they automatically pass

```lisp
(game "Flawless Pink Lorikeet – Forced Pass"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; skip turn when no move exists
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New element*: the **`force_pass`** clause tells the engine to automatically pass the turn when the mover cannot satisfy the destination constraint.

---

## 5️⃣  Add a **start‑position** – the board begins with two pre‑placed pieces (the “pink lorikeet” theme)

The board is indexed row‑major from 0.  
On a 5 × 5 board the centre squares are 12 (row 2, col 2) and 14 (row 2, col 4).  
We place **P1** on 12 and **P2** on 14 before the first turn.

```lisp
(game "Flawless Pink Lorikeet – With Start Position"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---- Initial placement -------------------------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (12))   ; centre square for P1
            (place P2 (14))   ; a square two steps away for P2
        )

        ;; ---- Normal play -------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        ;; ---- End of game -------------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    ;; Optional: give each player a colour (just for pretty rendering)
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*New element*: a **`start`** block with two `place` rules that seed the board before the first repeat‑phase begins.

---

### What’s left for a “full‑featured” version?

* **Capture / flip** mechanics (e.g., removing opponent pieces that become surrounded).  
* **Variable board size** or a **hexagonal** board.  
* **Special patterns** (e.g., “must create a line of exactly 3”).  

Those concepts would require additional masks, predicates, and possibly custom functions, but the core of *Flawless Pink Lorikeet* is already expressed in the final Ludax program above. Feel free to extend it further!

Below are **five incremental implementations** of the same game – **“Unique Marvellous Goldfish”** – each one adding a rule that is progressively harder to express in the Ludax language.  
All snippets are **stand‑alone, syntactically correct** Ludax programs and can be compiled/run independently.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – place‑anywhere, win by a line of 4  

*Board*: 5 × 5 square.  
*Play*: Players alternate placing a goldfish on any empty square.  
*End*: First player to make a line of four (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Unique Marvellous Goldfish – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)                     ; players alternate forever
                (place (destination empty))    ; may place on any empty cell
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))          ; 4‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add **capture** of adjacent opponent pieces & a **score** win condition  

*New mechanic*: When a piece is placed, every orthogonal/diagonal neighbour that belongs to the opponent is captured (removed) and the mover’s score is increased by 1 for each capture.  
*End*: A player who reaches **5 points** wins (in addition to the line‑of‑4 rule).

```lisp
(game "Unique Marvellous Goldfish – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; take any adjacent opponent piece
                            increment_score:true)                ; +1 per capture
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; first to 5 points wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Enforce **“Unique” placement** – cannot be placed next to an opponent piece  

*New constraint*: A goldfish may only be placed on an empty square **that is not orthogonally/diagonally adjacent to any opponent piece** at the moment of placement.  
(This makes each placement “unique” with respect to the opponent.)

```lisp
(game "Unique Marvellous Goldfish – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; stay away from opponent
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Add **initial placement** and a **flipping** mechanic (Reversi‑style)  

*Start rules*: Each player begins with a single goldfish placed in the centre of the board (square 5 × 5 → centre cells 12 and 13).  
*New effect*: After a normal placement, any straight line (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) of opponent pieces that is **bounded** by the newly placed piece and another of the mover’s pieces is **flipped** (ownership changes).  
*Score*: After flipping, both players’ scores are updated to the total number of pieces they own.

```lisp
(game "Unique Marvellous Goldfish – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))          ; centre left
            (place P2 (13))          ; centre right
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; keep the “unique” rule
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true)
                        (flip (custodial any))                     ; flip any bounded line
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))      ; update scores
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Why this is harder*:  
The **`flip`** effect uses the **`custodial`** mask, which requires the engine to look in all eight directions for a bounded line of opponent pieces – a non‑trivial pattern that cannot be expressed with a simple mask alone.

---

## 5️⃣  “Marvellous” win condition – line of 4 **must contain at least one “marvellous” piece**  

*Marvellous piece*: a goldfish that was placed on a square that, **at the moment of placement**, had **no adjacent pieces of either colour** (completely isolated).  
*New end rule*: A player wins only if they have a line of four **and** that line contains **at least one marvellous piece**.  
Because Ludax does not have a built‑in predicate for “piece placed on an empty‑neighbourhood”, we emulate it with a **state flag** using the score system:

1. When a piece is placed on a square with **no neighbours at all**, we give the mover **+10 temporary points** (a “marvellous token”).  
2. The end‑condition checks that the mover’s score is **≥ 10** *and* a line‑of‑4 exists.  
3. After a win, the extra points are ignored – they only serve to certify the presence of a marvellous piece.

> **Note:** This is deliberately a *work‑around* that pushes the limits of what can be expressed in pure Ludax. A true “piece‑property” predicate would require language extensions.

```lisp
(game "Unique Marvellous Goldfish – v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; unique rule
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true)

                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))

                        ; ----- Marvellous token -----
                        (increment_score mover
                            (if
                                (and
                                    (not (adjacent occupied))          ; no neighbours of any colour
                                    (not (adjacent (occupied opponent))) )
                                (add 10 0)                               ; give +10 points
                                0) )                                    ; otherwise +0
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            ; win only if line‑of‑4 *and* we have earned the marvellous token (≥10 extra points)
            (if (and (line 4) (>= (score mover) 10)) (mover win))

            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; fallback win by normal score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

### What makes this version “harder to express”?

* The **marvellous‑piece** concept is a *temporal* property (it depends on the board state **at the moment of placement**).  
* Ludax lacks a direct predicate like “the piece placed this turn had no neighbours”.  
* We therefore **encode** the property indirectly by awarding a large temporary score (+10) only when the placement satisfies the isolation condition, and later require that extra score for a win.  
* This workaround demonstrates how non‑trivial game‑design ideas can be forced into the existing grammar, highlighting its expressive limits.

---

### Summary of the incremental steps  

| Version | New feature introduced | Ludax construct that made it tricky |
|--------|-----------------------|--------------------------------------|
| v1 | Basic placement & line‑of‑4 win | None – straightforward |
| v2 | Capture + score increment | `effect_capture` with `increment_score:true` |
| v3 | “Unique” placement (no adjacent opponent) | Destination mask using `not (adjacent …)` |
| v4 | Start placement & flipping of bounded lines | `start` block, `effect_flip` with `custodial any`, score recomputation |
| v5 | “Marvellous” win condition (requires isolated piece) | No native predicate → simulated with a temporary score bonus and a composite end‑rule |

Feel free to copy any of the snippets into a Ludax compiler and experiment further – each version builds on the previous one while staying fully syntactically valid. Enjoy playing **Unique Marvellous Goldfish**!
