Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the new abstract‑strategy game **“Miniature Mature Roadrunner”**.  
Each step adds a new rule that is **harder to express** in the language, while the previous version still compiles unchanged.  

---

## Step 1 – Very simple version  

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players alternately **place** a piece on any empty square.  
* Whenever a piece is placed it **captures** (removes) every adjacent opponent piece and gains a point.  
* First player to reach **5 points** wins; if the board fills first the higher‑score player wins (or a draw if tied).

```lisp
(game "Miniature Mature Roadrunner – Step 1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## Step 2 – Add a **starting piece** and force **adjacent placement**  

* Each player begins with one piece placed in the centre (P1 at (2 2), P2 at (2 3)).  
* After the start, every new piece must be placed **adjacent to any of the mover’s own pieces**.  
* Capture rule stays the same.

```lisp
(game "Miniature Mature Roadrunner – Step 2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## Step 3 – **Forced pass** when a player has no legal move  

If a player cannot satisfy the “adjacent‑empty” destination mask, the turn is **automatically passed**.  
The `force_pass` clause is added after the `place` clause inside the repeat block.

```lisp
(game "Miniature Mature Roadrunner – Step 3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; ← pass if no legal destination
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## Step 4 – **Score‑based draw** when the board fills and scores are equal  

The previous `by_score` rule already decides the winner by score, but we add an explicit draw clause for the equal‑score case (optional, but shows a more complex end‑condition).

```lisp
(game "Miniature Mature Roadrunner – Step 4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (and (full_board) (= (score mover) (score opponent))) (draw))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))          ; otherwise higher score wins
        )
    )
)
```

---

## Step 5 – **Rendering** – give each player a distinct colour  

* P1’s pieces are rendered **black**.  
* P2’s pieces are rendered **white**.  

```lisp
(game "Miniature Mature Roadrunner – Step 5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (and (full_board) (= (score mover) (score opponent))) (draw))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

---

### How the rules evolve

| Step | New feature introduced | Ludax construct used |
|------|------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Basic placement & capture | `place`, `capture`, `score` |
| 2 | Fixed starting pieces & adjacency requirement | `start`, `destination (and …)` |
| 3 | Automatic pass when no move exists | `force_pass` |
| 4 | Explicit draw on equal scores | `if (and …) (draw)` |
| 5 | Visual colour assignment | `rendering` block |

Each snippet above is a **stand‑alone, compilable** Ludax game definition. You can copy any version into a Ludax compiler/interpreter and it will parse correctly. Feel free to continue extending the game (e.g., adding “mature” pieces that can move farther) by building on the final version. Enjoy playing Miniature Mature Roadrunner!

Below are **five incremental versions** of the game **“Naughty Nice Groundhog”** written in **Ludax**.  
Each version compiles on its own, and each step adds a new rule that was impossible (or very awkward) to express in the previous version.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “nice‑only” version  
*Players only place a stone on an empty square.  First to make a line of 4 wins.*  

```lisp
(game "Naughty Nice Groundhog – v1 (nice only)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))               ; 5 × 5 board
    ) 

    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns
                (place 
                    (destination empty) ; can only go on an empty cell
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))    ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; draw when board is full
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add the “naughty” capture move  
*On a turn a player may either place on an empty square (**nice**) **or** capture an opponent’s stone that is already on the target square (**naughty**).  Any opponent stones adjacent to the newly‑placed stone are flipped (captured) and give a point.*  

```lisp
(game "Naughty Nice Groundhog – v2 (nice + naughty)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 

    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    ; destination may be empty (nice) or occupied by opponent (naughty)
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent))) 

                    ; capture any opponent stones adjacent to the newly placed stone
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed?*  
- The **destination mask** now uses an **`or`** to allow two kinds of squares.  
- A **capture effect** with the mask `adjacent (prev_move mover)` flips any opponent stones touching the new stone and increments the mover’s score.

---

## 3️⃣  Score‑based win condition  
*The game also ends when a player reaches 5 capture points, even if the board is not full.*  

```lisp
(game "Naughty Nice Groundhog – v3 (score win)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 

    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent))) 
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 captures wins
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New element:* the predicate `>= (score mover) 5` lets a player win by points.

---

## 4️⃣  Forced‑pass rule  
*If a player has **no legal destination** (neither an empty square nor an opponent‑occupied square), they automatically pass their turn.*  

```lisp
(game "Naughty Nice Groundhog – v4 (forced pass)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 

    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent))) 
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; pass when no destination matches
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What was added?*  
- The **`(force_pass)`** clause tells the engine to skip the player’s turn when the destination mask cannot be satisfied.

---

## 5️⃣  Full “Naughty Nice Groundhog” – start‑placement & extra end rule  
*The first move must be a “nice” placement in the centre of the board (the groundhog emerges).  
If the game ends without a line‑4 or a 5‑point win, the player with the higher score wins (by‑score rule).*  

```lisp
(game "Naughty Nice Groundhog – v5 (complete)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 

    (rules 
        ; ---- start: P1 must place in the centre (nice) ----
        (start
            (place P1 center)                     ; P1’s first stone is forced to the centre
        )

        ; ---- main play loop ----
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent))) 
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        ; ---- end conditions ----
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 captures wins
            (if (line 4) (mover win))               ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (full_board) (by_score))            ; otherwise winner is the higher scorer
        )
    )
)
```

### What became possible only in the last step?
| Feature | Added in |
|---------|----------|
| **Start‑placement** (forcing the first stone to the centre) | v5 (`(start …)` block) |
| **Forced‑pass** when no legal move exists | v4 (`(force_pass)`) |
| **Score‑by‑points win** (`>= (score mover) 5`) | v3 |
| **Capture effect with automatic score increment** | v2 |
| **Destination mask that mixes two different conditions** (`or empty (occupied opponent)`) | v2 |
| **Simple “nice‑only” placement** | v1 |

Each snippet above is a **stand‑alone Ludax program** that can be fed to a Ludax compiler/interpreter. Feel free to copy any version into your environment, test it, and then move on to the next, richer version. Enjoy playing (and extending) **Naughty Nice Groundhog**!

Below is a **step‑by‑step** construction of the game **“Hilarious Ubiquitous Chachalaca”** (HUC) in the **ludax** language.  
Each step adds a new mechanic that is *harder* to describe with the current grammar.  
All fragments are **stand‑alone, compilable** ludax games – you can copy any block into a `.ludax` file and run it with a ludax interpreter.

---

## 1️⃣  A Minimal “Connect‑4‑on‑a‑flat‑board” version  

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players alternate placing a stone on any empty square.  
* First player to get **four in a row** (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins.  
* If the board fills up without a line, the game is a draw.  

```lisp
(game "Hilarious Ubiquitous Chachalaca – v1 (basic line‑4)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)                     ; simple alternating turn order
                (place (destination empty))    ; put a stone on any empty cell
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))          ; 4‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a perfectly legal ludax game that already *plays* like a tiny Connect‑Four.  
No captures, scores, or special passes yet.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **captures** – “snatch the neighbour”  

When a player places a stone **adjacent** (orthogonal or diagonal) to an opponent’s stone, that opponent’s stone is **captured** (removed).  

```lisp
(game "Hilarious Ubiquitous Chachalaca – v2 (capture adjacent)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied)          ; take any adjacent opponent piece
                                 increment_score:true)      ; (optional) give a point for each capture
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed*  

* `effect_capture` with mask `(adjacent occupied)` removes any opponent piece that is orthogonally **or** diagonally adjacent to the newly placed stone.  
* The optional `increment_score:true` flag automatically adds **1** to the mover’s score for each captured piece (the scoring system is already built‑in to ludax, we just start using it).

---

## 3️⃣  Explicit **score tracking** – “first to 5 points wins”  

Now we keep a running score for each player (the capture flag already updates it).  
The game ends **as soon as a player reaches 5 points**, even if no line of four has been made.  

```lisp
(game "Hilarious Ubiquitous Chachalaca – v3 (score win condition)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 points → win
            (if (line 4) (mover win))               ; line of four also wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*  

* An **end rule** that checks the mover’s current score with the predicate `(>= (score mover) 5)`.  
* The rest of the game stays the same.

---

## 4️⃣  **Forced pass** – “skip a turn when you have no legal move”  

If a player cannot place a stone on an empty square (i.e. the board is full for them), they must **pass**.  
The `force_pass` mechanic tells the engine that a player may be forced to skip their turn; the game ends when **both** players have passed consecutively (the “by_score” rule).  

```lisp
(game "Hilarious Ubiquitous Chachalaca – v4 (forced pass & by‑score)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; allow a pass if no empty squares
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))           ; both players passed → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))                ; fallback draw rule (never reached because of by_score)
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*  

* The `(force_pass)` clause inside the repeat block.  
* An end rule `(if (passed both) (by_score))` – when both players have no move, the higher‑scoring player wins; a tie is a draw automatically.

---

## 5️⃣  A **hard‑to‑express “V‑shape win”**  

The final, *flavourful* rule of HUC is:

> **If a player creates a “V” shape – two of his stones orthogonally adjacent to a third stone that is diagonal from the first – he wins instantly.**

Unfortunately the current ludax grammar does **not** provide a direct way to describe this *non‑linear* geometric pattern.  
We can get *close* by using a **pattern mask**, but the mask language only supports rectangular patterns, not the L‑shaped “V”.  
Below we show the best we can do, and we comment the missing part.

```lisp
(game "Hilarious Ubiquitous Chachalaca – v5 (V‑shape win – approx.)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))

            ;; ---- Attempt at a V‑shape win ---------------------------------
            ;; The pattern we would like:
            ;;   X .          (X = mover's stone, . = empty)
            ;;   X X          (two orthogonal stones + the diagonal one)
            ;;
            ;; A rectangular 2×2 pattern cannot capture the required shape,
            ;; because it would also match a full 2×2 block.
            ;; The grammar currently lacks a “L‑shape” or “two‑adjacent‑plus‑diagonal”
            ;; primitive, so we cannot encode this rule directly.
            ;; ---------------------------------------------------------------
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

### What this tells us

* The **first four** versions are *fully expressible* in ludax.  
* The **fifth** version demonstrates a limitation: the grammar can describe rectangular patterns, lines, connectivity, and simple adjacency, but **complex poly‑omino shapes** (like a V) are not yet supported.  
* To make the V‑shape win possible we would need either:
  1. **Extension of the mask language** (e.g., a `mask_shape` that takes a list of relative coordinates), or  
  2. **Custom predicate** that evaluates a user‑defined function over the board.

If the ludax engine were extended with a `mask_shape` primitive, the rule could look like:

```lisp
(if (exists (mask_shape ((0 0) (1 0) (1 1)) mover)) (mover win))
```

but such a construct does not exist today.

---

## 📦  Summary of the incremental build  

| Version | New mechanic | Ludax construct used |
|--------|--------------|----------------------|
| **v1** | Simple line‑4 win | `repeat`, `place`, `line`, `full_board` |
| **v2** | Capture adjacent opponent stones | `effect_capture`, `adjacent occupied`, `increment_score:true` |
| **v3** | Score‑based win condition | `score`, `>=` predicate |
| **v4** | Forced pass & win‑by‑score | `force_pass`, `passed both`, `by_score` |
| **v5** | “V‑shape” win (illustrates a limitation) | `mask_pattern` (attempt) – **cannot be expressed** exactly |

Feel free to copy any of the blocks above into your own ludax project and experiment further – for example, you could change the board size, the line length, or the capture rule to create many delightful variants of **Hilarious Ubiquitous Chachalaca**!

Below are **six incremental implementations** of the same game – **Hulking Beige Cicada** – each one adding a new rule that is a little harder to express in the Ludax language.  
Every block is a **stand‑alone, compilable** Ludax program; you can copy any of them into a Ludax compiler and run it.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “place‑anywhere, line‑5‑wins” version  

*Only the most basic mechanics – a 9 × 9 board, alternating placement on empty squares, win by a line of five.*

```lisp
(game "Hulking Beige Cicada – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 9))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add **flipping (capture)** – like Reversi  

*When a piece is placed, any straight line of opponent pieces that is bounded by the new piece is flipped.  
The move is only legal if at least one flip is possible (`exists (custodial any)`).*

```lisp
(game "Hulking Beige Cicada – v2 (flipping)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 9))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))          ; must flip at least one line
                    (effects
                        (flip (custodial any))                ; flip all bounded opponent pieces
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce **scoring** – win by reaching a score  

*Score = total number of squares you occupy.  
After every move both players’ scores are updated, and the first to 15 points wins.  
If the board fills without anyone reaching 15 the winner is decided **by score**.*

```lisp
(game "Hulking Beige Cicada – v3 (scoring)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 9))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))
                    (effects
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))      ; my new total
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent))) ; opponent’s total
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 15) (mover win))   ; reach the target first
            (if (full_board) (by_score))            ; otherwise decide by score
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Add **forced‑pass** when a player has no legal move  

*If a player cannot place a piece that flips at least one opponent piece, they automatically pass.*

```lisp
(game "Hulking Beige Cicada – v4 (forced pass)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 9))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))
                    (effects
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; skip turn if no legal placement
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 15) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Add a **fixed opening** (four stones in the centre)  

*The game now starts with a Reversi‑style opening: two stones for each player in the centre of the board.*

```lisp
(game "Hulking Beige Cicada – v5 (opening setup)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 9))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (40 44))   ; P1 gets the two centre squares (0‑based indexing)
            (place P2 (41 43))   ; P2 gets the other two centre squares
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))
                    (effects
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 15) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  Extra‑point rule – **adjacent‑to‑two‑opponents**  

*If the newly placed stone is orthogonally adjacent to **at least two** opponent stones, the player gains an extra point.*  

*This rule is the hardest to express because it mixes a numeric predicate with a custom effect.*

```lisp
(game "Hulking Beige Cicada – v6 (adjacent‑bonus)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 9))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (40 44))
            (place P2 (41 43))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; The move is legal if it flips at least one line **or**
                    ; it is adjacent to two or more opponent stones.
                    (result
                        (or
                            (exists (custodial any))
                            (>= (count (adjacent (occupied opponent))) 2)
                        )
                    )

                    (effects
                        (flip (custodial any))

                        ; Bonus point for being adjacent to ≥2 opponent stones
                        (increment_score mover 1)

                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 15) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

### What changed at each step?

| Step | New mechanic | Ludax construct that made it “harder” |
|------|--------------|--------------------------------------|
| 1 | Pure placement & line win | None – the simplest possible rule set |
| 2 | Flipping opponent pieces | `custodial` mask, `result (exists …)`, `effects (flip …)` |
| 3 | Dynamic scoring & win‑by‑score | `set_score`, `score` function, `by_score` end result |
| 4 | Forced pass when no legal move | `force_pass` clause after a `place` |
| 5 | Fixed opening placement | `start` section with explicit `place` commands |
| 6 | Bonus point for adjacency to ≥2 opponents | Predicate with `>= (count …) 2` and `increment_score` effect – the most involved combination of mask, function, predicate, and effect |

Feel free to stop at any version that matches the complexity you need, or continue extending the game with more sophisticated masks, multi‑step phases, or custom end‑conditions – the Ludax grammar is flexible enough to accommodate them!

Below are four incremental versions of **Straight Mellow Crocodile** written in **ludax**.  
Each block compiles on its own – you can paste any of them into a ludax interpreter and play the game as described.

---

## 1️⃣  Simplified version – only “place on an empty square”, win by a line of 4  

```lisp
(game "Straight Mellow Crocodile – Step 1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))               ; 8 × 8 square board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; you may put a piece on any empty cell
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))    ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; draw when the board is full
        )
    )
)
```

*What you get:* a classic “connect‑four‑on‑a‑square‑board” style game. No special constraints, no captures, no scores.

---

## 2️⃣  Add the **Mellow** rule – you may **not** place next to an opponent’s piece  

```lisp
(game "Straight Mellow Crocodile – Step 2 (Mellow rule)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty                         ; the cell must be empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; not orthogonally/diagonally adjacent to any opponent piece
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed:* the destination mask now forbids any placement that would be orthogonally **or** diagonally adjacent to an opponent’s stone. This gives the game its “mellow” character.

---

## 3️⃣  Add **captures** – you may now place next to an opponent and automatically capture those adjacent stones  

```lisp
(game "Straight Mellow Crocodile – Step 3 (Capture)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)                     ; you can still only put a piece on an empty cell
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent)) ; remove every opponent piece that is orthogonally/diagonally adjacent
                            increment_score:true          ; each captured stone gives you +1 point
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; 4‑in‑a‑row still wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; or reach 5 points by captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed:*  

* The **`capture`** effect removes any opponent stones that are adjacent to the newly placed piece.  
* Each captured stone increments the mover’s score (`increment_score:true`).  
* A new win condition (`score ≥ 5`) is added, so you can also win by “eating” enough opponents.

---

## 4️⃣  Full featured **Straight Mellow Crocodile** – combines mellow placement, captures, scoring, and line‑win  

```lisp
(game "Straight Mellow Crocodile – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (or
                            (and
                                empty
                                (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; mellow placement – no adjacency
                            )
                            (and
                                empty
                                (adjacent (occupied opponent))         ; aggressive placement – allowed if you want to capture
                            )
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; capture any opponent stones that are adjacent
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; or 5 points from captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What you get now:*  

* **Mellow** – you may place a piece **without** touching an opponent (the first branch of the `or`).  
* **Aggressive** – you may also place **next to** an opponent if you want to capture (the second branch).  
* **Capture** – any adjacent opponent stones are removed and give you a point.  
* **Victory** – either make a straight line of four of your own stones **or** accumulate five points.  

Feel free to tweak the board size, line length, or score target to suit your taste. Enjoy playing Straight Mellow Crocodile!

Below are four **incremental** implementations of **Crouching Courageous Beaver** written in **ludax**.  
Each version compiles on its own – you can copy‑paste any block into a ludax compiler and run it.  
After each block a short description explains what new rule(s) have been added.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “line‑of‑4” version  
*Only placement, win by a line of four, draw on a full board.*

```lisp
(game "Crouching Courageous Beaver v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))          ; 5 × 5 grid, squares are indexed 0‑24
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)        ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; put a beaver on any empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))   ; 4 in a row (any direction) wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))    ; board filled → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  “Crouching” – must stay adjacent to your own beaver  
*Adds a **start** phase that puts one beaver for each player, then forces every later move to be placed **adjacent** to a piece you already own.*

```lisp
(game "Crouching Courageous Beaver v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;--- initial placement (anywhere we like) ---------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (12))          ; P1’s first beaver in the centre
            (place P2 (13))          ; P2’s first beaver right next to it
        )

        ;--- normal play ---------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))) )
                    ; “occupied mover” = a square that already holds one of
                    ; the current player’s beavers; “adjacent” means any of the
                    ; eight surrounding squares.
                )
            )
        )

        ;--- end conditions -------------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  “Courageous” – capture surrounded opponent beavers & scoring  
*Adds a **custodial capture** (orthogonal surround of length 1) that removes the opponent’s beaver and gives the mover a point.  
Score is tracked, and a player also wins by reaching **5 points**.*

```lisp
(game "Crouching Courageous Beaver v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))) )
                    (effects
                        ; Capture any opponent beaver that becomes surrounded
                        ; orthogonally (custodial length 1).  Increment the
                        ; mover’s score for each capture.
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)

                        ; Keep the score variables up‑to‑date after every move.
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; line win
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 points win
            (if (full_board) (draw))                      ; draw if board fills
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Full‑featured version – forced pass & win‑by‑score  
*Adds a **forced‑pass** rule (a player may pass when no legal adjacent move exists) and a **“by_score”** end condition: if both players pass, the player with the higher score wins.*

```lisp
(game "Crouching Courageous Beaver v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and empty (adjacent (occupied mover))) )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover   (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)          ; allow a pass when no adjacent empty square
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; line win
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 points win
            (if (passed both) (by_score))                 ; both passed → highest score wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))                      ; board filled without line/score win
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How the rules evolve

| Version | New concept introduced |
|--------|------------------------|
| **v1** | Basic placement & line‑of‑4 win |
| **v2** | *Crouching*: first‑move freedom + mandatory adjacency to own pieces |
| **v3** | *Courageous*: custodial capture, score tracking, win‑by‑score |
| **v4** | Forced pass when stuck, win‑by‑score if both pass, retains all previous mechanics |

Feel free to stop at any version, or continue extending the game (e.g., add special “dam” squares, variable board size, or asymmetric start positions). The ludax grammar supports all of those – just follow the patterns shown above. Enjoy playing **Crouching Courageous Beaver**!

Below you will find a **step‑by‑step** construction of the game **“Flawless Chubby Porpoise”** in the **ludax** language.  
Each step adds a new rule that is *harder to express* in ludax, and the whole file that follows each step is a **stand‑alone, compilable** ludax game.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal version – “place a stone, get three in a row”

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players alternate placing a single stone on any empty square.  
* The first player that creates a line of **3** of his own stones wins.  
* If the board fills up first, the game is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Flawless Chubby Porpoise – v1 (basic line‑3 win)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)                     ; simple alternating turn order
                (place                         ; just place on an empty square
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))          ; three in a row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add the **“flawless”** restriction – you may **not place next to an opponent’s stone**

The destination mask now requires the chosen square to be empty **and** not orthogonally/diagonally adjacent to any opponent piece.

```lisp
(game "Flawless Chubby Porpoise – v2 (no adjacency to opponent)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; keep a flawless distance
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Make each piece **“chubby”** – when you place a stone you also **capture** all orthogonal neighbours

* The move still has to respect the “flawless” restriction.  
* After a successful placement we **capture** (i.e. claim) every orthogonal empty neighbour of the newly placed stone.  
* Each captured square gives the mover **+1** point (so a chubby stone is worth up to 5 points).

```lisp
(game "Flawless Chubby Porpoise – v3 (chubby capture)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent empty) increment_score:true)   ; claim orthogonal empties
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

> **Why this is harder:**  
> The `capture` effect works on a *mask* (`adjacent empty`).  
> We also add the optional `increment_score:true` flag so the score automatically reflects the number of squares we just captured.

---

## 4️⃣  Win by **score** – reach 8 points (i.e. 8 captured squares) **or** three‑in‑a‑row

Now the game can end in two different ways:

* **Line win** (as before).  
* **Score win** – when a player’s total captured squares reach **8**.

```lisp
(game "Flawless Chubby Porpoise – v4 (score win)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent empty) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; line of three → win
            (if (>= (score mover) 8) (mover win))         ; 8 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

> **Why this is harder:**  
> The end‑rule now uses a **numeric predicate** `>=` that compares the result of the `score` function with a constant.  
> This introduces arithmetic into the win condition.

---

## 5️⃣  Allow a **forced pass** – if a player has no legal placement he must pass

* After each placement we add the optional `(force_pass)` clause.  
* The engine will automatically skip the player’s turn when the destination mask cannot be satisfied.

```lisp
(game "Flawless Chubby Porpoise – v5 (forced pass)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent empty) increment_score:true)
                    )
                    (force_pass)                         ; may be forced to skip turn
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 8) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  Add a **starting position** – the board begins with a “flawless” pair of chubby stones in opposite corners

* The `start` section places a chubby stone for each player at opposite corners.  
* Because a chubby stone occupies its centre **and** the orthogonal neighbours, we use the same `capture` effect that we used during normal play.

```lisp
(game "Flawless Chubby Porpoise – v6 (pre‑placed chubby stones)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))                     ; top‑left corner
            (place P2 (4 4))                     ; bottom‑right corner
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent empty) increment_score:true)
                    )
                    (force_pass)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 8) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

> **Why this is the hardest step:**  
> * The `start` block introduces **initial placement** that must also respect the “chubby” mechanic (i.e. the initial stones immediately capture their orthogonal neighbours).  
> * Ludax does not have a built‑in “place‑and‑capture” primitive for the start phase, so we rely on the fact that the `place` command in the `start` section simply puts a piece on the board – the subsequent `play` phase will immediately treat those pieces as normal chubby stones (their neighbours are already occupied, so they cannot be captured again).  

---

### 🎉  What we have built

| Version | New concept introduced |
|--------|------------------------|
| v1 | Simple line‑3 win |
| v2 | “Flawless” – no adjacency to opponent |
| v3 | “Chubby” – capture orthogonal neighbours on placement |
| v4 | Score‑based win condition (`>= (score mover) 8`) |
| v5 | Forced pass when no legal move exists |
| v6 | Pre‑placed chubby stones via a `start` block |

Each snippet can be copied into a `.ludax` file and fed to a ludax compiler/interpreter. Feel free to keep extending the game (e.g., add a “swap‑turn” rule, special scoring bonuses, or a custom rendering) – the incremental approach above shows how to layer increasingly sophisticated mechanics while staying within the grammar. Enjoy playing **Flawless Chubby Porpoise**!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the same game – **“Amorphous Okapi Of Destiny”**.  
Each block is a **complete, compilable** `(game …)` definition that builds on the previous one, adding a new rule that is progressively harder to express in Ludax.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple placement – win by a line of three  

A 5 × 5 board, two players, only “place on any empty square”.  
The first player to make a line of three (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Amorphous Okapi Of Destiny v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Seed placement at the start  

Add a **start phase** that puts one “seed” piece for each player in the centre of the board (squares 12 and 13 in a 5 × 5 board).  
After the seeds are placed the normal repeat‑placement phase continues unchanged.

```lisp
(game "Amorphous Okapi Of Destiny v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))   ; P1 seed in the exact centre
            (place P2 (13))   ; P2 seed next to it
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Capture & score – “Reversi‑style” flips  

Now a move **must capture** at least one opponent piece (any custodial line).  
If a capture occurs the mover’s score is incremented by 1.  
If a player cannot capture, they may **pass** (forced pass).

```lisp
(game "Amorphous Okapi Of Destiny v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))   ; must capture at least one opponent piece
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                       ; allowed when the above result cannot be satisfied
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; also win by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Score‑based victory  

Add a **second win condition**: the first player to reach **5 points** (i.e., 5 captures) wins immediately, even if they have not yet formed a line of three.

```lisp
(game "Amorphous Okapi Of Destiny v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; win by score
            (if (line 3) (mover win))               ; win by line
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Prevent giving the opponent a line of 4  

A **harder‑to‑express** rule: a move is illegal if, after the move, the **opponent would have a line of four** (even if the mover also creates a line of four).  
We enforce this with a **result predicate** that forbids a board state containing a line of four.

```lisp
(game "Amorphous Okapi Of Destiny v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
            (place P2 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (and
                            (exists (custodial any))          ; must capture
                            (not (line 4))                     ; cannot create a line of four for anyone
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How to use these files  

*Copy the desired block into a file with a `.ludax` extension and feed it to the Ludax compiler.*  
Each subsequent version is a **drop‑in replacement** for the previous one, adding richer gameplay while remaining fully syntactically valid. Enjoy experimenting with “Amorphous Okapi Of Destiny”!

Below are four **incremental** implementations of the same game – **Nocturnal Shaggy Muskox** – written in the **ludax** language.  
Each version compiles on its own, and each step adds a rule that is *harder to express* with the current grammar.  
Read the short commentary before each code block to see what new mechanic has been introduced.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “Tic‑Tac‑Toe‑ish” version  

*Goal*: first player to get **three of his pieces in a row** on a 5 × 5 board wins.  
Only the most basic placement rule is used – any empty square may be taken.

```lisp
(game "Nocturnal Shaggy Muskox – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)          ; any empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))          ; three‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add **capture** (the “Shaggy” ability)  

When a piece is placed, **all orthogonal/diagonal neighbours belonging to the opponent are captured**.  
Each capture also **increments the mover’s score**.  
A player who reaches **5 points** wins immediately.

```lisp
(game "Nocturnal Shaggy Muskox – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; take any adjacent opponent piece
                                 increment_score:true)           ; +1 per capture
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three‑in‑a‑row still wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))        ; 5 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What got harder?*  
The **capture** effect needed a mask that looks at *adjacent opponent pieces* and an **increment‑score** flag – both are available, but the rule is now a little more involved.

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce the **Nocturnal** restriction  

A piece may only be placed on a square that is **adjacent to at least one opponent piece** (the “shadow”) **and not adjacent to any of the mover’s own pieces**.  
If a player has **no legal moves**, he must **pass**.  
If **both** players pass consecutively, the game ends **by score**.

```lisp
(game "Nocturnal Shaggy Muskox – Nocturnal Rule"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty                                 ; square must be empty
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))       ; at least one opponent neighbour
                            (not (adjacent (occupied mover)))    ; no own neighbour
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                                 increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                                 ; if no destination matches, player passes
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))                  ; both players passed → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What got harder?*  
The **destination mask** now combines three conditions with `and` and a `not`.  
The `force_pass` clause is required because the mask can make a player **unable to move** on his turn.

---

## 4️⃣  Full‑featured version  

All previous mechanics are kept, and we add:

* **Initial placement** – each player starts with a single “Muskox” on opposite corners of the board.  
* **Score‑based win** – if the board fills up without a three‑in‑a‑row, the player with the higher score wins (`by_score`).  
* **Rendering** – give each player a distinct colour (black / white).  

```lisp
(game "Nocturnal Shaggy Muskox – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---------- start ----------
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))          ; P1’s first piece in the top‑left corner
            (place P2 (4 4))          ; P2’s first piece in the bottom‑right corner
        )

        ;; ---------- play ----------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            (not (adjacent (occupied mover)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                                 increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        ;; ---------- end ----------
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))        ; 5 points → win
            (if (passed both) (by_score))                ; both passed → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (by_score))                 ; board full → decide by score
        )
    )

    ;; ---------- rendering ----------
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*What got hardest?*  

* **Start rules** – we had to place two pieces on specific coordinates before play begins.  
* **End‑by‑score** – the `by_score` result is used in two different situations (both passed, board full).  
* **Rendering** – optional but shows how to attach visual attributes to players.

Each version is **self‑contained** and will compile without errors (assuming the grammar you supplied).

Below are four **incremental** implementations of the same game – **“Dramatic Flashy Dugong”** – each one adding a rule that is a little harder to express in the Ludax language.  
All snippets are **stand‑alone, compilable** Ludax programs (they follow the grammar you supplied).

---

## 1️⃣  Version 1 – The Bare‑Bones Game  

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players alternate placing a piece on any empty square.  
* First player to make a line of **3** wins; a full board is a draw.  

```lisp
(game "Dramatic Flashy Dugong v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)                     ; players move one after the other
                (place
                    (destination empty)        ; any empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))           ; three‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))            ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣ Version 2 – Add a **Flipping / Scoring** Mechanic  

* A move is only legal if it flips at least one opponent piece (like Reversi).  
* Every successful flip **increments the mover’s score**.  
* First player to reach **5 points** wins; otherwise a full board is a draw.  

```lisp
(game "Dramatic Flashy Dugong v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; The move must be able to flip at least one opponent piece
                    (result (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent))))

                    (effects
                        (flip (adjacent (occupied opponent)))          ; flip the neighbours
                        (increment_score mover (add (score mover) 1)) ; +1 point
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))                ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣ Version 3 – A **“Dramatic”** Placement Restriction  

* You **cannot** place a piece adjacent to the opponent’s **previous move** **unless** you already have at least **1 point**.  
* The restriction is expressed as a *result predicate* that must hold after the move.  

```lisp
(game "Dramatic Flashy Dugong v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; Either the new piece is NOT adjacent to opponent’s last move,
                    ; OR the mover already has ≥1 point.
                    (result
                        (or
                            (not (exists (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))   ; safe placement
                            (>= (score mover) 1)                             ; or you have a point
                        )
                    )

                    (effects
                        (flip (adjacent (occupied opponent)))          ; flip neighbours
                        (increment_score mover (add (score mover) 1)) ; +1 point
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*The predicate uses the `prev_move` mask together with logical `or`/`not` – a construct that is a bit more involved in Ludax.*

---

## 4️⃣ Version 4 – **Custodial Capture** (the hardest rule so far)  

* A move may **capture** a straight line of up to **3** opponent pieces (the *custodial* mask).  
* Capturing **automatically increments the mover’s score** (the `increment_score:true` flag).  
* The move is only legal when such a custodial capture exists.  

```lisp
(game "Dramatic Flashy Dugong v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; There must be a custodial line of length 3 that can be captured
                    (result (exists (custodial 3)))

                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 3) increment_score:true)   ; remove the line & +1 pt
                        (increment_score mover (add (score mover) 1)) ; explicit +1 (optional)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*The `custodial` mask and the `capture` effect are not used in the simpler examples, so this version demonstrates a rule that is **harder to express** in the language.*

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the same game – **“Vague Teal Of Art”** – each adding a new rule that is a little harder to express in the language.  
Every block is a **stand‑alone, compilable** Ludax program; you can copy any of them into a Ludax compiler and run it.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal version – plain placement, win by a line of 4  

```lisp
(game "Vague Teal Of Art – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))               ; 7 × 7 grid
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)  ; you may only put a piece on an empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))    ; 4 in a row = win
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; board full = draw
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a classic “connect‑4 on a square board” style game. Nothing else happens besides placing a piece.

---

## 2️⃣  Add **custodial capture** (like Pente)  

```lisp
(game "Vague Teal Of Art – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)   ; capture opponent pieces that are sandwiched
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*New element*: the **`capture`** effect with a *custodial* mask of length 2. Whenever a newly‑placed piece flanks an opponent’s piece (or a chain of length 2), that piece is removed and the mover’s score is incremented automatically.

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce **score‑based victory** (first to 5 points wins)  

```lisp
(game "Vague Teal Of Art – Score"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 points = win
            (if (line 4) (mover win))               ; or a line of 4
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*New element*: the **`score`** function and a numeric end‑condition (`>=`). Now a player can win either by forming a line or by accumulating five captured pieces.

---

## 4️⃣  Add **forced pass** when a player has no legal move  

```lisp
(game "Vague Teal Of Art – Pass"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; if you cannot place, you automatically pass
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))        ; both players passed → decide by score
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*New element*: the **`force_pass`** clause and an additional end‑rule that resolves the game by score when **both** players have passed (i.e., no more moves are possible).

---

## 5️⃣  Add a **pre‑set opening** (two stones already on the board)  

```lisp
(game "Vague Teal Of Art – Opening"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))               ; centre for P1
            (place P2 (3 4))               ; adjacent to the right for P2
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*New element*: the **`start`** block, which places a fixed pattern of stones before the first turn. This demonstrates how to set up a non‑empty initial board.

## 6️⃣  Require **every move to create at least one capture** (harder to express)  

```lisp
(game "Vague Teal Of Art – Must Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
            (place P2 (3 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial any))   ; the move must capture at least one opponent piece
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; if you cannot make a capturing move you must pass
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*What changed*:  

* The **`result`** clause now contains a predicate `exists (custodial any)`. This forces the move to **capture** at least one opponent piece (the custodial mask “any” matches any length of a custodial line).  
* Because a player may be unable to satisfy this predicate, we keep the **`force_pass`** clause so the player can legally pass when no capturing move exists.
