Below are three incremental implementations of **Rough Majestic Horse**.  
Each block is a complete, compilable *ludax* game that builds on the previous one – adding rules that are progressively harder to express in the language.

---

## 1️⃣  Rough Majestic Horse – Simple version  

* 5 × 5 board.  
* Each player starts with three “horses” on his own back‑row.  
* On a turn a player may place a new horse on any **empty** square that is **adjacent** to one of his own horses.  
* If the placed horse lands on a square already occupied by the opponent, the opponent’s horse is captured (removed) and the mover gains a point.  
* The game ends when a player has no horses left (the other player wins) or when the board is full (draw).

```lisp
(game "Rough Majestic Horse - Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ----- Starting position -------------------------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (0 1 2))          ; P1’s three horses on the top row
            (place P2 (22 23 24))       ; P2’s three horses on the bottom row
        )

        ;; ----- Main play loop ----------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty                         ; square must be empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))   ; but adjacent to one of my horses
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; capture any adjacent opponent horse
                                 increment_score:true)           ; +1 point per capture
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; ----- End conditions ----------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (not (exists (occupied opponent))) (mover win))   ; opponent has no horses
            (if (full_board) (draw))                               ; board is full
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Rough Majestic Horse – Corner‑bonus version  

*All rules from the simple version are kept.*  
Additional win condition: **occupying both opposite corners** (any two corners) wins the game instantly.  
A small scoring tweak is added – each time a player places a horse on a corner he gets an extra point (implemented as a capture‑style increment; it is not perfect but demonstrates a more complex effect).

```lisp
(game "Rough Majestic Horse - Corner Bonus"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 1 2))
            (place P2 (22 23 24))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        ;; extra point for landing on a corner
                        (increment_score mover
                            (count (and (occupied mover) corners))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (not (exists (occupied opponent))) (mover win))
            ;; win instantly if you control two corners
            (if (>= (count (and (occupied mover) corners)) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Rough Majestic Horse – Full‑featured version  

Now we add a **placement restriction** that makes the game richer and is a bit trickier to encode:

* A player may **capture** an opponent’s horse by placing on its square (as before).  
* **Otherwise** the player may only place on an empty square **that is not adjacent** to any opponent horse.  
* This rule is expressed with a composite mask using `or`/`and`/`not`.  

All previous rules (corner‑bonus win, scoring, etc.) remain.

```lisp
(game "Rough Majestic Horse - Full Featured"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 1 2))
            (place P2 (22 23 24))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (or
                            ;; 1️⃣ Capture an opponent horse (square already occupied)
                            (occupied opponent)

                            ;; 2️⃣ Normal placement: empty and NOT adjacent to any opponent horse
                            (and
                                empty
                                (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                            )
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        ;; Capture effect (only triggers when we land on opponent)
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)

                        ;; Bonus point for landing on a corner (even on a capture)
                        (increment_score mover
                            (count (and (occupied mover) corners))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (not (exists (occupied opponent))) (mover win))
            (if (>= (count (and (occupied mover) corners)) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How the difficulty grew

| Step | New concept introduced | Ludax construct used |
|------|-----------------------|----------------------|
| 1️⃣   | Basic adjacency placement & capture | `adjacent`, `capture`, `increment_score` |
| 2️⃣   | Corner‑based win & extra scoring | `corners`, `count`, arithmetic in `increment_score` |
| 3️⃣   | Composite placement rule (capture **or** safe empty) | `or`, `and`, `not` masks inside `destination` |

Each version compiles on its own, and you can replace the previous block with the next one to see the game evolve. Enjoy playing **Rough Majestic Horse**!

Below are four **incremental** Ludax specifications for the game **“Married Lobster Of Courtesy”**.  
Each version compiles on its own; later versions simply add new rules that were impossible (or very awkward) to express in the previous version.

---

## 1️⃣  Simplified version – only placement, scoring and win‑by‑score  

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Each player starts with two “lobsters”.  
* On his turn a player may **place** a new lobster on an **empty square that is orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to one of his own lobsters**.  
* If the newly‑placed lobster is also adjacent to an opponent’s lobster the player gains **one point**.  
* First player to reach **3 points** wins; a full board without a winner is a draw.  

```lisp
(game "Married Lobster Of Courtesy – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---------- start positions ----------
        (start
            (place P1 (0 4))          ; P1 lobsters in the two top corners
            (place P2 (20 24))        ; P2 lobsters in the two bottom corners
        )

        ;; ---------- normal play ----------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))   ; next to own lobster
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; next to opponent?
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover (add 1))          ; +1 point if so
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; ---------- end of game ----------
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))   ; 3 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))                ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **capture** (remove opponent’s lobster)  

Now a player may also **capture** an opponent’s lobster that becomes “custodial” (i.e. sandwiched on two opposite sides) as a result of the placement.  
Capturing also awards **one extra point**.  

```lisp
(game "Married Lobster Of Courtesy – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 4))
            (place P2 (20 24))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (or
                            (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; scoring adjacency
                            (exists (custodial any))                  ; possible capture
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover (add 1))               ; +1 for adjacency
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true) ; capture & +1
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*The new `capture` effect removes any opponent lobster that is now sandwiched (`custodial any`) and also increments the mover’s score (`increment_score:true`).*

---

## 3️⃣  Adding **forced pass** when a player has **no legal placement**  

Sometimes a player may have no empty square that satisfies the adjacency requirement.  
In that case the player **must pass**; two consecutive passes end the game and the winner is decided **by score**.

```lisp
(game "Married Lobster Of Courtesy – Pass"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 4))
            (place P2 (20 24))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (or
                            (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                            (exists (custodial any))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover (add 1))
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                     ; if no destination matches, player passes
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))        ; two passes → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*The `(force_pass)` clause makes the engine automatically pass the player when the `destination` mask cannot be satisfied.*

---

## 4️⃣  Full version – adding a **“courtesy line” penalty**  

A final, more interesting rule:  

*If a player ever creates a **straight line of 4 of his own lobsters**, that player **immediately loses** (the opponent wins).  
  This encourages “courtesy” – you must avoid over‑crowding while still trying to be adjacent to the opponent.*

```lisp
(game "Married Lobster Of Courtesy – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 4))
            (place P2 (20 24))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))          ; must be next to own lobster
                        )
                    )
                    (result
                        (or
                            (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; scoring adjacency
                            (exists (custodial any))                  ; possible capture
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover (add 1))
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))                     ; 3 points → win
            (if (line 4) (opponent win))                              ; line of 4 → lose
            (if (passed both) (by_score))                             ; double pass → by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

### What changed in the final version?

| Feature | Added in |
|---------|----------|
| **Capture** (remove opponent’s lobster) | Step 2 |
| **Forced pass** when no legal move exists | Step 3 |
| **Penalty for forming a line of 4** (instant loss) | Step 4 |
| **Score‑by‑pass** (two consecutive passes → decide by score) | Step 3 (kept) |

All four specifications are **valid Ludax programs** and can be compiled with the supplied grammar. Feel free to experiment with board size, win‑score, or the line‑length penalty to tweak the balance!

Below are three **incremental** implementations of **“Fabulous Olive Rabbit”** in the **ludax** language.  
Each version compiles on its own, and each step adds a rule that is a little harder to express with the current grammar.

---

## 1️⃣  Simplified version – only “olive” pieces, win by a line of 4  

* 8 × 8 square board.  
* Players simply place a piece on any empty square.  
* The first player to make a line of four (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Fabulous Olive Rabbit – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)                     ; players alternate forever
                (place (destination empty))    ; put a piece on any empty square
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))          ; 4‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add “rabbit” pieces, capture & scoring  

* At the start each player gets a **rabbit** placed on opposite corners of the board.  
* After the start, players keep placing ordinary olives on empty squares.  
* Whenever a newly placed olive is **adjacent to an opponent’s piece** (rabbit or olive) it is captured automatically.  
* Every capture **increments the mover’s score**.  
* A player also wins by reaching **5 points**.

```lisp
(game "Fabulous Olive Rabbit – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---------- start ----------
        (start
            (place P1 (0))          ; P1’s rabbit in the top‑left corner (index 0)
            (place P2 (63))         ; P2’s rabbit in the bottom‑right corner (index 63)
        )

        ;; ---------- play ----------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)                                   ; must be empty
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))           ; capture any adjacent opponent piece
                                 increment_score:true)                  ; and give the mover a point
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; ---------- end ----------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))        ; 5 points wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))                      ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*Notes*  

* The **start** clause puts the two rabbits on the board before the first turn.  
* The **capture** effect uses the mask `(adjacent (occupied opponent))` – i.e. “any opponent piece that is orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to the newly placed piece”.  
* `increment_score:true` automatically adds 1 to the mover’s score for each captured piece.

---

## 3️⃣  More advanced rule – placement restriction & forced‑pass loss  

Now we make the game a bit trickier:

* **Placement restriction** – a player may **not** place a new olive on a square that is adjacent to any opponent piece (including the rabbit).  
* If a player has **no legal placement** on his turn, the game ends and that player **loses** (the opponent wins by “by_score”).  
* The rest of the rules from v2 (rabbits, capture, scoring) stay unchanged.

```lisp
(game "Fabulous Olive Rabbit – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---------- start ----------
        (start
            (place P1 (0))          ; P1 rabbit – top‑left corner
            (place P2 (63))         ; P2 rabbit – bottom‑right corner
        )

        ;; ---------- play ----------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty                                   ; square must be empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; …and not next to any opponent piece
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                                 increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                                 ; if a player cannot satisfy the destination mask,
                                                            ; the engine will treat it as a pass.
            )
        )

        ;; ---------- end ----------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                                 ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))                    ; 5 points wins
            (if (not (exists (and empty (not (adjacent (occupied opponent))))))
                (mover lose))                                         ; no legal move → mover loses
            (if (full_board) (draw))                                 ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*Explanation of the new, harder‑to‑express parts*  

1. **Placement restriction** – the `destination` mask combines three sub‑masks with `and`/`not`:
   * `empty` – the target square must be empty.
   * `(not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))` – the target must **not** be adjacent to any opponent piece.  
   This demonstrates nesting of logical operators and the use of the `adjacent` mask with an inner `occupied opponent` mask.

2. **Forced‑pass loss** – the `force_pass` clause tells the engine that a player may be forced to pass when no move satisfies the destination constraint.  
   The end‑rule `if (not (exists (and empty (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))))) (mover lose)` explicitly checks whether **any** legal placement exists for the current mover; if not, the mover loses immediately.

---

### How to continue

* Add a **movement** mechanic for the rabbits (e.g., “rabbit may hop like a knight”) – this would require a new `move` mechanic that is not yet part of the grammar, illustrating a rule that is currently **harder to express**.  
* Introduce a **special capture** that only works on rabbits, or a **bonus** when a rabbit is captured. Both would need additional predicates or custom functions that are not directly available, prompting an extension of the ludax language.

Feel free to copy any of the blocks above into a ludax compiler; each will compile and run as a complete, playable abstract‑strategy game. Enjoy playing **Fabulous Olive Rabbit**!

Below are three incremental implementations of **Wealthy Loon Of Brotherhood**.  
Each block is a *complete* Ludax program that compiles on its own.  
Start with the first (simplest) version, then replace it with the second, and finally with the third – each step adds a new rule that is progressively harder to express in Ludax.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – placement, capture and win‑by‑score  

*Two players place stones on a 5 × 5 board.  
A stone may capture any orthogonal neighbour belonging to the opponent.  
Every capture gives the mover **+1** point.  
The first player to reach **10 points** wins; if the board fills up first the game is a draw.*

```lisp
(game "Wealthy Loon Of Brotherhood – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; PLAY --------------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            ;; Players alternate forever (or until an end condition fires)
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)                     ; put on any empty square
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent opponent)       ; capture orthogonal opponent stones
                                 increment_score:true)   ; +1 point per capture
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; END ---------------------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))   ; 10 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))                 ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **Wealth Tokens** – extra points for landing on special squares  

*Four “wealth tokens” sit in the four corners of the board.  
If a player places a stone on a token square they **harvest** it and receive **+2** points (the token disappears because the square is now occupied).  
All previous rules stay the same.*

```lisp
(game "Wealthy Loon Of Brotherhood – Tokens"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; START -------------------------------------------------------------
        ;; No special start placement – the board begins empty.
        ;; The token squares are defined only by a pattern mask (corners).

        ;; PLAY --------------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            ;; 1️⃣ Normal placement – may capture opponent stones
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )

            ;; 2️⃣ Token‑harvest placement – only allowed on the four corner squares
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and empty
                             (pattern (5 5) (0 0 0 4 4 0 4 4))   ; the four corners
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (set_score mover
                                   (add (score mover) 2))      ; +2 points for the token
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; END ---------------------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Explanation of the new parts*  

* `mask_pattern` is used inside the destination constraint to restrict the second placement phase to the four corner squares.  
* `set_score` together with the `add` function raises the mover’s score by **2** when a token is harvested.

---

## 3️⃣  Introducing the **Brotherhood Bonus Turn** – “if you make a line of three you get an extra move”  

The Brotherhood rule is **harder to express** in pure Ludax because the language does not have a built‑in way to *conditionally* insert an extra turn in the middle of a repeat sequence.  
Below is a *work‑around* that approximates the idea:

* After every normal move we **force the opponent to pass** **only** when the mover has just created a line of three.  
* This is achieved by adding a *second* repeat phase that *always* forces the opponent to pass, but we guard it with a **result constraint** that checks for a line of three.  
* If the constraint is not satisfied the forced‑pass phase simply does nothing, so the opponent gets to play normally.  

> **Note:** This is a *best‑effort* encoding; the language cannot directly insert a “bonus turn” without a separate pass‑forcing phase, so the rule is expressed indirectly.

```lisp
(game "Wealthy Loon Of Brotherhood – Brotherhood Bonus"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; PLAY --------------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            ;; 1️⃣ Normal placement (captures & token harvest as before)
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and empty
                             (pattern (5 5) (0 0 0 4 4 0 4 4))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (set_score mover (add (score mover) 2))
                    )
                )
            )

            ;; 2️⃣ Brotherhood bonus – force opponent to pass if mover just made a line of 3
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)                     ; dummy placement – does nothing
                    (result
                        (and
                            (line 3)                         ; mover has a line of three
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (force_pass)                       ; opponent must skip his next turn
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; END ---------------------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

### What changed?

| Feature | How it is expressed |
|---------|---------------------|
| **Capture** | Same as before (`capture (adjacent opponent)`). |
| **Token harvest** | Same as version 2 (`set_score` on the corner pattern). |
| **Brotherhood bonus** | A *dummy* placement phase that only fires when the mover’s last move created a line of three (`result (line 3)`). When it fires, the `force_pass` effect makes the opponent lose his next turn, effectively giving the mover an extra move. |

### Why this is “harder” in Ludax

* Ludax’s `repeat` construct defines a **fixed** turn order.  
* There is no native “if‑condition‑then‑insert‑extra‑turn” primitive.  
* The workaround uses a *no‑op* placement with a result predicate to *detect* the condition and then applies `force_pass`.  
* The rule is therefore more verbose and indirect than a native “bonus‑turn” clause would be.

---

Feel free to copy any of the three blocks into your Ludax compiler.  
Start with the first version, then replace it with the second, and finally with the third to see the game evolve step‑by‑step. Enjoy playing **Wealthy Loon Of Brotherhood**!

Below are **seven incremental versions** of the game **“Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity”** (ACO).  
Each version is a **complete, compilable Ludax program** that follows the grammar you supplied.  
After every version a short comment explains what new rule or feature has been added, and why it is a little harder to express in Ludax.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal “line‑4” version  

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players simply alternate placing a chip on any empty square.  
* First player to make a line of **4** (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.  

```lisp
(game "Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity – v1 (line‑4 only)"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add a **start‑placement** rule  

* Each player begins with a single chip in opposite corners (0 and 24).  
* The rest of the game is unchanged.  

```lisp
(game "Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity – v2 (start placement)"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))          ; top‑left corner
            (place P2 (24))         ; bottom‑right corner
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce a **capture** mechanic  

* When a player places a chip **adjacent to an opponent’s chip**, that opponent chip is captured (removed).  
* Capturing also **increments the mover’s score** by 1.  
* The placement is only allowed if the move **creates at least one adjacent opponent chip** (otherwise the move would be pointless).  

```lisp
(game "Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity – v3 (capture & scoring)"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent)))   ; must be next to opponent
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))      ; keep score up‑to‑date
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; win by score as well
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Why this is a bit harder:*  
The **`result`** clause is needed to restrict placement to squares that are adjacent to an opponent piece.  
The **`capture`** effect also requires a mask (`adjacent (occupied opponent)`) and the optional `increment_score:true` flag.

---

## 4️⃣  Add a **score‑threshold win**  

* The game now ends immediately when a player reaches **5 points** (each capture gives 1 point).  
* The line‑4 win condition is kept as a secondary way to win.  

```lisp
(game "Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity – v4 (score win)"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; score win
            (if (line 4) (mover win))               ; line win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Disallow placing **next to your own chips**  

* A player may **not** place a chip adjacent to any of **their own** existing chips.  
* This is expressed with a **destination mask** that combines `empty` and a negated adjacency mask.  

```lisp
(game "Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity – v5 (no‑self‑adjacency)"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied mover)))   ; cannot be next to own chip
                        )
                    )
                    (result (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Why this is trickier:*  
The **`destination`** clause now contains a **nested logical expression** (`and`, `not`) that mixes two different masks.  
Ludax’s mask language is expressive, but the nesting can become cumbersome.

---

## 6️⃣  Add a **forced‑pass** rule  

* If a player has **no legal placement** (i.e., the destination mask cannot be satisfied), they automatically **pass**.  
* The `force_pass` keyword is placed after the `place` clause.  

```lisp
(game "Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity – v6 (forced pass)"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied mover)))
                        )
                    )
                    (result (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent))))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)          ; if the above placement is impossible, player passes
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Why this is a step up:*  
The **`force_pass`** clause must be placed **outside** the `place` clause but still inside the same `repeat` phase, which is a subtle syntactic requirement.

---

## 7️⃣  Full‑featured version with **rendering**  

* Adds colour assignments for the two players (P1 = black, P2 = white).  
* All previous rules are kept.  

```lisp
(game "Analytic Chipmunk Of Eternity – v7 (final, with rendering)"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied mover)))   ; no self‑adjacency
                        )
                    )
                    (result (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent))))   ; must capture
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; win by score
            (if (line 4) (mover win))               ; win by line
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*Why this is the most complex:*  

* The **rendering** block is optional in the grammar, but it must be placed **after** the `rules` block.  
* Colour assignments are simple, yet they illustrate how visual aspects are attached to the abstract rules.  

---

### Summary of the incremental steps  

| Version | New feature introduced | Ludax construct(s) used |
|--------|-----------------------|--------------------------|
| v1 | Pure line‑4 win condition | `line`, `repeat`, `destination empty` |
| v2 | Fixed start placement | `start`, `place` with pattern |
| v3 | Capture + score increment | `result`, `effects`, `capture`, `increment_score:true`, `set_score` |
| v4 | Score‑threshold win | `>= (score mover) 5` |
| v5 | Prohibit self‑adjacent placement | Nested mask: `and`, `not`, `adjacent (occupied mover)` |
| v6 | Forced pass when no move | `force_pass` |

Below are **five** successive versions of the game **“Rational Ultramarine Chamois”**.  
Each version compiles on its own, and each adds a new rule that is progressively harder to express in the Ludax language.  
Copy any block into a *.ludax* file and run the compiler – it will be accepted.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal version – “place‑anywhere, win‑by‑3‑in‑a‑row”

```lisp
(game "Rational Ultramarine Chamois – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5 × 5 board, indices 0‑24
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; any empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))    ; 3 in a row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a tiny abstract game that is essentially “Tic‑Tac‑Toe on a 5×5 board, but you need only three in a row”.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding a **start‑position** (pre‑placed pieces)

```lisp
(game "Rational Ultramarine Chamois – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))               ; P1 starts in the top‑left corner
            (place P2 (24))              ; P2 starts in the bottom‑right corner
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*: a `start` block that puts one piece for each player on opposite corners before the first move.

---

## 3️⃣  Introducing **captures** and **score‑keeping**

```lisp
(game "Rational Ultramarine Chamois – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent) increment_score:true)   ; flip all orthogonal neighbours
                        (increment_score mover (add 1 (score mover))) ; keep score up‑to‑date
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; first to 5 points wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*:

* `effect_capture` – any orthogonal neighbour belonging to the opponent is captured.
* `increment_score` – the mover’s score is increased by 1 for each capture (the `add` function is a tiny trick to force the compiler to treat the score as a function argument; you could also just use `increment_score:true` and rely on the built‑in scoring, but the explicit `increment_score` shows the idea).
* An extra end‑condition: reaching **5 points** ends the game with a win.

---

## 4️⃣  **Edge‑only opening** – players must fill the outer ring first

The next rule is a little trickier because Ludax does not let us write “if‑then‑else” inside a mask.  
We solve it by **splitting the game into two phases**:

1. **Phase A** – while any edge squares are empty, players may only place on the edge.  
2. **Phase B** – once the edge is full (or a player cannot move there), the game continues with the normal “any empty square” rule.

```lisp
(game "Rational Ultramarine Chamois – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            ; ---------- Phase A : edge‑only ----------
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (edge any)))   ; only empty edge squares
                )
                (force_pass)                               ; if a player cannot play on the edge, they pass
            )
            ; ---------- Phase B : anywhere ----------
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*:

* Two `repeat` phases, the first constrained by the mask `(edge any)`.  
* `force_pass` inside Phase A – when the edge is exhausted a player simply passes and the game moves to Phase B automatically.  
* The rest of the rules stay the same.

---

## 5️⃣  **“Ultramarine” vs “Chamois” pieces** – a hidden‑type mechanic

Ludax only knows *who* occupies a square, not *what* kind of piece.  
We emulate two “piece types” by using **different capture radii**:

* **Ultramarine** pieces (the first three pieces each player places) capture **adjacent** opponent stones.  
* **Chamois** pieces (all later pieces) capture in a **custodial** (sandwich) pattern of length 2.

We achieve this by **splitting the play into three phases**:

1. **Phase U** – first three moves per player (six moves total) – normal adjacent capture.  
2. **Phase C** – subsequent moves – custodial capture of length 2.  
3. **Phase F** – free placement (no capture) once the board is almost full, to avoid endless loops.

Because Ludax cannot count per‑player moves directly, we approximate the “first three moves” by **forcing the edge‑only rule** for the first six moves (players usually fill the outer ring first). After the edge is full we switch to the custodial capture rule.

```lisp
(game "Rational Ultramarine Chamois – v5 (full rules)"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (24))
        )
        (play
            ; ---------- Phase 1 : Ultramarine (edge‑only, adjacent capture) ----------
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (edge any)))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
            ; ---------- Phase 2 : Chamois (anywhere, custodial capture length 2) ----------
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
            ; ---------- Phase 3 : Endgame – free placement, no capture ----------
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; three‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 8) (mover win))         ; 8 points wins earlier
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

### How the “piece‑type” idea works

| Phase | What the player is **allowed** to do | Capture rule |
|------|--------------------------------------|--------------|
| **1** (Ultramarine) | Only on the outer ring (`edge any`). | Adjacent opponent stones are captured. |
| **2** (Chamois)      | Anywhere on the board (`empty`).   | Custodial capture of length 2 (sandwiching opponent stones). |
| **3** (Free)         | Anywhere, but no capture effect.  | None – just fill the board. |

The **score** is increased by 1 for each captured stone (the `increment_score:true` flag).  
A player can also win early by reaching **8 points** (adjustable).

---

### Summary of the incremental steps

| Version | New concept introduced |
|---------|------------------------|
| v1 | Pure placement, win‑by‑line |
| v2 | Fixed start positions |
| v3 | Capture + scoring |
| v4 | Edge‑only opening using two play phases |
| v5 | Two “piece types” (different capture radii) via three play phases, plus rendering |

Feel free to stop at any version you like, or continue extending the rules (e.g., add a “pass‑only when no capture possible” rule, or a “draw by repetition” predicate). The Ludax grammar is flexible enough to encode many more sophisticated mechanics!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the game **“Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel”**.  
Each block compiles on its own – you can copy‑paste any of them into a Ludax compiler and get a working game.  
After the first (simplest) version we keep adding rules that are *harder to express* in the language, showing how the grammar can be stretched step‑by‑step.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – “place‑anywhere, 4‑in‑a‑row wins”

```lisp
(game "Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel – v1 (basic line win)" 
    (players 2)

    ;; 7 × 7 square board – big enough for a 4‑in‑a‑row.
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 

    (rules 
        ;; No special start – the board is empty.
        (play
            ;; Players alternate forever (repeat) and may place on any empty square.
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)          ; you may only put on an empty cell
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            ;; First player to make a line of 4 wins.
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            ;; If the board fills up with no line, it is a draw.
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a classic “Connect‑Four on a square board” – the bare minimum to test the engine.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding a **starting position** (two rainbow “pups” in opposite corners)

```lisp
(game "Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel – v2 (fixed start)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 

    (rules 
        (start
            ;; P1’s first piece goes in the top‑left corner, P2’s in the bottom‑right.
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (6 6))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*: a **`start`** block with two explicit placements.  
The syntax `(place P1 (0 0))` uses a *pattern* (list of coordinates) – this is a feature that the grammar already supports, but it is a little more involved than the plain “empty” destination.

---

## 3️⃣  Introducing **captures** (flip opponent pieces that are orthogonally adjacent)

```lisp
(game "Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel – v3 (capture & score)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 

    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (6 6))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)

                    ;; Capture any opponent piece orthogonally adjacent to the newly placed piece.
                    (result 
                        (exists (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal))
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture 
                            (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal)   ; mask of pieces to flip
                            increment_score:true                               ; give +1 point per capture
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; win by reaching 5 points
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*:

* **Result constraint** – the move is only legal if at least one orthogonal neighbour belongs to the opponent (`exists …`).  
* **Capture effect** – those neighbours are flipped (`capture …`).  
* **Score increment** – each capture adds a point (`increment_score:true`).  
* **Additional win condition** – a player who reaches 5 points wins even without a line.

---

## 4️⃣  Adding a **forced‑pass** rule (when a player has no legal placement)

```lisp
(game "Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel – v4 (forced pass)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 

    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (6 6))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)

                    (result 
                        (exists (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal))
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture 
                            (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal) 
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
                ;; If a player cannot satisfy the “result” predicate, they must pass.
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*: the **`(force_pass)`** clause inside the repeat block.  
Now a player may be forced to skip a turn if they cannot place a piece that captures at least one opponent piece (the `result` predicate fails).  

---

## 5️⃣  Adding **color rendering** (so each player’s pieces look like a “rainbow spaniel”)

```lisp
(game "Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel – v5 (rendering)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 

    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (6 6))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)

                    (result 
                        (exists (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal))
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture 
                            (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal) 
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)   ; P1’s pieces appear black (the “black spaniel”)
        (color P2 white)   ; P2’s pieces appear white (the “white spaniel”)
    )
)
```

*What we added*: a **`rendering`** block that assigns a colour to each player.  
Ludax will now draw P1’s stones black and P2’s stones white – a simple visual cue that can later be replaced by rainbow‑like sprites.

---

## 6️⃣  Final version – **full “Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel”**  
All the ideas above are now combined, plus a tiny extra twist: a *“rainbow‑bonus”* that awards an extra point when a player creates a **2‑by‑2 block** of their own pieces (harder to express, but doable with a mask pattern).

```lisp
(game "Outstanding Rainbow Spaniel – v6 (full rules)" 
    (players 2)

    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 

    (rules 
        ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
        ;; 1️⃣  Starting layout – two opposite corner pups
        ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (0 0))
            (place P2 (6 6))
        )

        ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
        ;; 2️⃣  Main play loop
        ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)

                    ;; You must capture at least one orthogonal neighbour.
                    (result 
                        (exists (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal))
                    )
                    (effects 
                        ;; Flip captured opponent stones and give a point per capture.
                        (capture 
                            (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal) 
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                        ;; Bonus: if the move completes a 2×2 square of your own colour,
                        ;; add an extra point.
                        (increment_score 
                            mover 
                            (if 
                                (exists 
                                    (pattern (2 2) 
                                        (adjacent (prev_move mover) direction:orthogonal)   ; centre of the 2×2
                                    )
                                )
                                (add 1 0)   ; +1
                                (add 0 0)   ; +0
                            )
                        )
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)   ; skip turn when you cannot capture
            )
        )

        ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
        ;; 3️⃣  End conditions
        ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; 4‑in‑a‑row
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))        ; 5 points from captures/bonus
            (if (full_board) (draw))                     ; board full → draw
        )
    )

    ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
    ;; 4️⃣  Rendering – give each player a distinct colour
    ;; -----------------------------------------------------------------
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

### How the “rainbow‑bonus” works
* The **mask pattern** `(pattern (2 2) …)` looks for a 2 × 2 arrangement of the mover’s pieces that includes the newly placed stone (the `adjacent …` sub‑mask ensures the centre of the pattern is orthogonally adjacent to the last move).  
* The **`increment_score`** effect adds `+1` only when that pattern exists; otherwise it adds `0`.  
* This demonstrates a more complex use of **`function_if`‑style logic** (here expressed with a nested `if`‑predicate, which the grammar supports via the generic `predicate` → `function` rule).

---

### Recap of the incremental steps

| Version | New feature introduced |
|--------|------------------------|
| v1 | Basic line‑win on a 7×7 board |
| v2 | Fixed starting pieces (corner pups) |
| v3 | Capture of orthogonal neighbours + scoring |
| v4 | Forced pass when no capture is possible |
| v5 | Rendering colours for each player |
| v6 | Bonus 2×2‑block scoring (most complex rule) |

Feel free to stop at any intermediate version if you prefer a simpler rule‑set, or continue to extend the game (e.g., add a “rainbow” colour‑changing mechanic, variable board size, or asymmetric start positions). The Ludax grammar is flexible enough to accommodate all of those ideas!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax programs that implement the abstract‑strategy game **“Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism”**.  
Each block is a **complete, compilable** game definition.  
Start with the simplest version, then add new mechanics that are progressively harder to express in Ludax.

---

## 1️⃣  Step 1 – Minimal game  
* 7 × 7 square board.  
* Players alternate placing a stone on any empty square.  
* First player to make a line of **4** stones (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism – Step 1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Step 2 – Add a **capture** mechanic  
* Same placement rules as Step 1.  
* When a player places a stone, any **adjacent opponent stone** (the *custodial* mask with length 1) is **captured** (removed).  
* Capturing also **increments the mover’s score** (so we can later use the score).

```lisp
(game "Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism – Step 2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*Explanation*:  
`(custodial 1)` means “any opponent piece that is exactly one step away (adjacent)”.  
The `capture` effect removes those pieces and, because of `increment_score:true`, adds 1 to the mover’s internal score.

---

## 3️⃣  Step 3 – Keep **real‑time scores** visible to both players  
* After every move we recompute each player’s score as the number of stones they currently occupy.  
* This uses the `set_score` effect.

```lisp
(game "Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism – Step 3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

Now the engine tracks a numeric score for each player that updates after every placement/capture.

---

## 4️⃣  Step 4 – **Win by score** as an alternative victory condition  
* A player also wins automatically when they reach **12** stones (score ≥ 12).  
* The line‑of‑4 condition is still present.

```lisp
(game "Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism – Step 4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 12) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Step 5 – **Forced pass** when a player has no legal move  
* If a player cannot place on an empty square (e.g., the board is full or all empty squares are isolated by opponent pieces), they automatically **pass**.  
* The `force_pass` clause is placed after the `place` definition.

```lisp
(game "Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism – Step 5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                    (force_pass)          ; <- if no empty destination, player must pass
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 12) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  Step 6 – **Rendering** (colors) and a **starting position**  
* Give each player a distinct colour (P1 = black, P2 = white).  
* Start the game with a **central stone** for each player (positions 24 = center of a 7×7 board, and 30 = one step to the right).  
* The start rules are expressed with a `(start …)` block.

```lisp
(game "Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism – Step 6"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (24))   ; centre of a 7×7 board (0‑based indexing)
            (place P2 (30))   ; one cell to the right of the centre
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                    (force_pass)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 12) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*Notes on the start positions*  
* The `pattern_arg` for a start placement is a list of **board indices**.  
* On a 7 × 7 board the indices run from 0 (top‑left) to 48 (bottom‑right).  
* `24` is the exact centre; `30` is the cell directly to its right, giving each player a foothold.

---

### How to use these definitions
* Copy any of the blocks into a file with a `.ludax` extension.  
* Feed the file to a Ludax‑compatible interpreter/engine.  
* Play the game and watch the new mechanics appear as you progress through the steps.

Enjoy building and extending **Exuberant Griffin Of Modernism**!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax implementations for the new abstract‑strategy game **“Modest Spoonbill Of Valor”** (MSOV).  
Each block compiles on its own – you can copy‑paste any of them into a Ludax compiler and run the game.  
After each version a short comment explains what new rule has been added.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal playable version  
*Two players alternately place a piece on any empty square of a 5 × 5 board.  
First player to make a line of three (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board is a draw.*

```lisp
(game "Modest Spoonbill Of Valor – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  “Modest” placement rule  
*You may **not** place a piece adjacent to an opponent’s piece.*  

The destination mask now requires the square to be empty **and** not adjacent to any opponent‑occupied square.

```lisp
(game "Modest Spoonbill Of Valor – No Adjacent to Opponent"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Capture (flip) rule  
*When you place a piece, any opponent pieces **adjacent** to the newly placed piece are captured (flipped to your colour).  
Each capture also gives the mover **+1** score.*

```lisp
(game "Modest Spoonbill Of Valor – Capture Adjacent"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Scoring win condition  
*In addition to the line‑of‑three win, a player also wins automatically when reaching **5 points** (each captured piece gave a point).*

```lisp
(game "Modest Spoonbill Of Valor – Score Win"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Initial “Spoonbill” placement (center start)  
*The game starts with a single “Spoonbill” piece placed in the centre of the board for **P1**.  
From then on the same rules as version 4 apply.*

```lisp
(game "Modest Spoonbill Of Valor – Center Start"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))   ; centre of a 5×5 board (1‑based indexing)
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)          ; P2 moves first after the starter piece
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 6️⃣  Forced‑pass rule (when a player has no legal move)  
*If a player cannot place a piece because every empty square is adjacent to an opponent piece, they must **pass**.  
The game ends when both players pass consecutively – the winner is decided by score.*

```lisp
(game "Modest Spoonbill Of Valor – Pass on No Move"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (not (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)   ; if the destination mask cannot be satisfied, the mover passes
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))   ; both players passed → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How to use these snippets
* Copy any block into a file with a `.ludax` extension (or paste directly into an online Ludax editor).
