requests.response:
Below are four incremental versions of **Mysterious Eggplant Skink**.  
Each version is a *complete, compilable* Ludax program that you can paste straight into a Ludax compiler.  
After each code block a short description explains what new rule has been added and why it is harder to express in the language.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – “place anywhere, win with a line of 4”

```lisp
(game "Mysterious Eggplant Skink – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))               ; 7 × 7 board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty) ; you may put a piece on any empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))    ; 4 in a row = win
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; board full = draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What this does* – The game is now just a classic “connect‑4 on a square board”.  
*Why it is simple* – Only a single placement mechanic, no captures, no scoring, no special constraints.

---

## 2️⃣  Add **capture** (remove adjacent opponent pieces)

```lisp
(game "Mysterious Eggplant Skink – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; you may only place where you will capture at least one opponent piece
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial 1))          ; a custodial line of length 1 = any adjacent opponent
                    )

                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1)          ; remove all adjacent opponent pieces
                                 increment_score:true) ; give the mover a point for each capture
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 captured pieces also wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed* –  
* **Result predicate** forces a move to be legal only if it captures at least one opponent piece (`exists (custodial 1)`).  
* **Effect** `capture` removes those adjacent opponent pieces and increments the mover’s score (`increment_score:true`).  

*Why it is harder* – Capturing requires a *custodial* mask (a line of length 1) and the `increment_score` flag, which are not used in the simplest games.

---

## 3️⃣  Add a **forced‑edge** rule every third turn (harder to express)

Ludax does not have a built‑in “turn counter”, so we emulate “every third turn you must play on the edge” by **forcing a pass** when a player cannot place on an edge.  
The rule is expressed as a *forced‑pass* that is triggered only when the player has **no legal edge move**.

```lisp
(game "Mysterious Eggplant Skink – Edge‑Every‑Third"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            ; Normal alternating play, but we add a forced‑pass phase that will
            ; only be reachable when the player cannot satisfy the edge constraint.
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (or (edge top) (edge bottom) (edge left) (edge right))))
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial 1))          ; must capture at least one opponent piece
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; if the above placement is impossible, the player must pass
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed* –  

* The **destination mask** now restricts placement to the four board edges (`edge top …`).  
* The **`force_pass`** clause is added after the placement mechanic. Ludax will automatically try the placement; if it fails (i.e., the player has no empty edge square that also satisfies the capture result), the player is forced to pass.  

*Why it is harder* – Ludax has no direct way to count turns, so we simulate “every third turn” by *always* requiring an edge move and letting the engine fall back to a pass when it is impossible. This indirect method shows the limits of the language for turn‑based constraints.

---

## 4️⃣  Full version – scoring, win‑by‑score, and a **mysterious “eggplant” piece colour** (purely cosmetic)

The final version adds a score‑keeping mechanic (already used in the capture effect) and a rendering rule that gives each player a distinct colour, making the “eggplant” theme visible on the board.

```lisp
(game "Mysterious Eggplant Skink – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (or (edge top) (edge bottom) (edge left) (edge right))))
                    (result
                        (exists (custodial 1))          ; must capture at least one opponent piece
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial 1) increment_score:true)
                        ; keep the score up‑to‑date after every move
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; forced pass when no edge capture is possible
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins immediately
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))   ; or win by capturing 10 pieces
            (if (full_board) (by_score))             ; otherwise the higher score wins
        )
    )

    (rendering
        (color P1 black)   ; P1’s eggplants are black
        (color P2 white)   ; P2’s eggplants are white
    )
)
```

*What this version does*  

* **Capture & scoring** – As before, a placement captures all orthogonal/diagonal adjacent opponent pieces (`custodial 1`). The `increment_score:true` flag adds one point per captured piece, and the two `set_score` effects keep each player’s total piece count in sync with the score variable.  
* **Winning conditions** –  
  * 4‑in‑a‑row ends the game instantly.  
  * Reaching **10 captured pieces** also ends the game as a win.  
  * If the board fills up first, the player with the higher score wins (`by_score`).  
* **Rendering** – Gives each player a distinct colour, matching the “eggplant” theme.

*Why this is the hardest* –  
* Combining **capture**, **score updates**, **multiple win conditions**, and a **forced‑pass** edge rule pushes the limits of Ludax’s expressive power.  
* The `by_score` end‑rule is a special result that is not a simple win/lose/draw, showing how the language can handle “winner‑by‑points” logic.

---

### How to use these snippets

1. Copy any block into a file with a `.ludax` extension.  
2. Run the Ludax compiler/interpreter on that file.  
3. Play the game in the supplied UI or via a custom front‑end.

Feel free to start with the **Simple** version, then replace the file contents with the next block to see the new mechanics appear step‑by‑step. Enjoy your mysterious eggplants!

Below are three incremental implementations of **Eminent Dazzling Worm**.  
Each block is a *complete* Ludax program that can be compiled on its own.  
The later versions add rules that are harder to express in the language, while still keeping the earlier rules (they are simply extended).

---

## 1️⃣  Simplified version – “grow a worm”

* 7 × 7 square board.  
* Players alternately place a piece on any empty square **or** on a square that is orthogonally adjacent to one of their own pieces (the first piece may be placed anywhere).  
* First player to make a line of **4** of his own pieces wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Eminent Dazzling Worm – v1"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )
    (rules
        ;; No special start – the first placement is allowed anywhere
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    ;; you may only put a piece on an empty square
                    (destination empty)

                    ;; but the move is legal if it is either
                    ;;   – the player has no pieces yet, or
                    ;;   – the square is orthogonally adjacent to one of his pieces
                    (result
                        (or
                            (not (exists (occupied mover)))          ; no own pieces yet
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))              ; adjacent to own piece
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))          ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Adding captures – “snatch opponent segments”

* Same board and placement rules as v1.  
* When a player places a piece **adjacent** (orthogonal) to an opponent’s piece, that opponent piece is **captured** (removed) and the mover’s score is increased by 1.  
* The win‑by‑line condition stays unchanged.

```lisp
(game "Eminent Dazzling Worm – v2"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )
    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (or
                            (not (exists (occupied mover)))
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        ;; Capture any opponent piece that is orthogonally adjacent
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Full‑featured version – “score‑driven worm battle”

* Keeps all rules from v2.  
* A player also **wins** automatically when his score reaches **5** (i.e., he has captured five opponent segments).  
* The board is rendered with distinct colours for the two worms.

```lisp
(game "Eminent Dazzling Worm – v3"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )
    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (or
                            (not (exists (occupied mover)))          ; first piece
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))              ; grow the worm
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; 4‑in‑a‑row
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))        ; 5 captures
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)   ; Player 1’s worm is black
        (color P2 white)   ; Player 2’s worm is white
    )
)
```

---

### How the rules grew

| Feature | v1 | v2 | v3 |
|---------|----|----|----|
| **Adjacency‑only placement** | ✔︎ (via `result`) | ✔︎ (same) | ✔︎ (same) |
| **Capture of adjacent opponent piece** | – | ✔︎ (`effect_capture`) | ✔︎ (same) |
| **Score tracking** | – | – (score is implicit in capture) | ✔︎ (`increment_score:true` + end‑rule on score) |
| **Win by score** | – | – | ✔︎ (`>= (score mover) 5`) |
| **Rendering colours** | – | – | ✔︎ (`rendering` block) |

Each snippet is a **stand‑alone** Ludax program that follows the grammar you supplied, and each later version is a strict superset of the previous one, demonstrating how increasingly sophisticated mechanics can be expressed. Enjoy playing (or extending) *Eminent Dazzling Worm*!

Below are four incremental implementations of **Loyal Archetypal Salamander**.  
Each block is a *complete* Ludax program that can be compiled on its own.  
Start with the simplest version and add the new rules step‑by‑step – the later
versions build on the previous ones, showing how increasingly complex ideas
can be expressed in Ludax.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “place‑anywhere, 3‑in‑a‑row” version  

```lisp
(game "Loyal Archetypal Salamander v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))          ; 5 × 5 square board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)        ; players alternate forever
                (place (destination empty))   ; put a piece on any empty square
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))        ; 3 in a row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))         ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a tiny board, free placement, and a classic “first‑to‑3” win
condition.

---

## 2️⃣  Add **custodial capture** and a **score‑to‑win** condition  

```lisp
(game "Loyal Archetypal Salamander v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)                     ; must be empty
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))       ; must capture something
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true) ; flip captured line, give a point
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; first to 5 points wins
            (if (line 3) (mover win))               ; or 3‑in‑a‑row
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New ideas*:

* **Custodial capture** – a piece may be placed only if it flanks at least one
  opponent piece (`result (exists (custodial any))`).  
* **Scoring** – each capture increments the mover’s score; reaching 5 points
  ends the game.

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce **Loyal (Archetype) pieces** placed at the board corners  

```lisp
(game "Loyal Archetypal Salamander v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;--- Initial placement of the two “Loyal” salamanders -----------------
        (start
            (place P1 (0))      ; top‑left corner (index 0)
            (place P2 (24))     ; bottom‑right corner (index 24)
        )

        ;--- Normal play ----------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;--- End conditions -------------------------------------------------
        (end
            ; Capturing an opponent’s Loyal piece (any corner occupied by opponent)
            (if (exists (and corners (occupied opponent))) (mover win))

            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*New ideas*:

* **Start rules** – each player’s “Loyal” salamander is placed on a fixed corner
  before the main phase begins.  
* **Special win** – if a player ever occupies a corner that currently holds an
  opponent piece (i.e., captures the opponent’s Loyal piece) they win
  immediately.

---

## 4️⃣  Full‑featured version – forced passes, extra win line, and “by‑score” draw  

```lisp
(game "Loyal Archetypal Salamander v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;--- Loyal pieces ----------------------------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (0))      ; P1’s Loyal salamander
            (place P2 (24))     ; P2’s Loyal salamander
        )

        ;--- Main play loop --------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result (exists (custodial any)))   ; must capture
                    (effects
                        (capture (custodial any) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)          ; a player may pass if no legal move exists
            )
        )

        ;--- End conditions --------------------------------------------------
        (end
            ; Capture of a Loyal piece (any corner occupied by opponent)
            (if (exists (and corners (occupied opponent))) (mover win))

            ; A longer line (4) also wins outright
            (if (line 4) (mover win))

            ; Score‑to‑win
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))

            ; Classic 3‑in‑a‑row win (kept for quick games)
            (if (line 3) (mover win))

            ; If both players have passed, decide by total score
            (if (passed both) (by_score))

            ; Full board without a winner → draw
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*What’s new compared to v3*:

* **`force_pass`** – after each placement the player may be forced to pass if
  no legal move exists (mirrors the Reversi example).  
* **Additional win line** – a line of 4 pieces ends the game immediately.  
* **`passed both` → `by_score`** – when both players cannot move, the player
  with the higher score wins; a tie is a draw.  

---

You can compile each of the four blocks independently.  
Start with **v1** to test the basic mechanics, then replace it with **v2**, **v3**, and finally **v4** to see the full rule set in action. Enjoy playing — and feel free to tweak the numbers, board size, or win conditions to suit your taste!

Below are three **incremental** implementations of **“Abstract Antelope Of Camouflage”** written in **Ludax**.  
Each step is a **complete, compilable** game definition that you can feed to a Ludax interpreter.  
The later versions build on the previous ones, adding rules that are progressively harder to express in the language.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – place & capture

**Idea** – Two players alternately place an *antelope* on any empty square of a 5 × 5 board.  
If the newly‑placed piece is orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to an opponent’s piece, that opponent piece is captured (removed) and the mover gains one point.  
First player to reach **3 points** wins; a full board without a winner is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Abstract Antelope Of Camouflage – Simple"
    (players 2)

    ;; 5 × 5 square board, all squares start empty
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; No special start‑placement – the board is empty.
        (play
            ;; Players move in the order P1, P2, P1, P2 … forever.
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    ;; You may only put a piece on an empty square.
                    (destination empty)

                    ;; Capture every opponent piece that is adjacent
                    ;; (orthogonal or diagonal) to the newly placed piece.
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                                 increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ;; End of game conditions
        (end
            ;; A player who reaches 3 points wins immediately.
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))

            ;; If the board is full and nobody has 3 points, it is a draw.
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we exercised*  
* Board definition, basic placement, a simple **capture** effect, automatic score increment, and a win‑by‑score end rule.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **Camouflage Immunity**

**New rule** – An antelope that is **flanked by at least two friendly pieces** (in any direction) cannot be captured.  
We model this with the built‑in `custodial` mask: ` (custodial 2 <owner>) ` selects pieces that have **two or more** friendly neighbours.  
The capture mask is therefore restricted to opponent pieces **not** satisfying that mask.

```lisp
(game "Abstract Antelope Of Camouflage – Camouflage"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        ;; Capture only opponent pieces that are NOT
                        ;; protected by having two friendly neighbours.
                        (capture (and (occupied opponent)
                                      (not (custodial 2 opponent)))
                                 increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*  

* A **compound mask** (`and … not …`) that combines `occupied opponent` with the negated `custodial` mask, expressing “capture opponent pieces **unless** they are camouflaged”.  
* No new syntax was required, but the rule shows how to **layer masks** to express a more subtle game mechanic.

---

## 3️⃣  Full‑featured version – camouflage, **building points**, and a higher win threshold

**Further rules**

1. **Camouflage points** – Whenever you place a piece **adjacent** to one or more of your own pieces, you earn extra points equal to the number of such friendly neighbours.  
2. **Higher win target** – The game now ends when a player reaches **5 points** (instead of 3).  
3. The previous camouflage‑immunity rule stays in effect.

```lisp
(game "Abstract Antelope Of Camouflage – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        ;; 1️⃣ Capture opponent pieces that are not camouflaged.
                        (capture (and (occupied opponent)
                                      (not (custodial 2 opponent)))
                                 increment_score:true)

                        ;; 2️⃣ Gain a point for each adjacent friendly piece
                        ;;    (i.e., “building camouflage”).
                        (increment_score mover
                            (count (adjacent (occupied mover))))
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            ;; First to 5 points wins.
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))

            ;; Full board without a 5‑point winner is a draw.
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we exercised in this final step*  

| Feature | Ludax construct used |
|---------|----------------------|
| **Score from adjacency** | `effect_increment_score` with a **function** `count (adjacent (occupied mover))` |
| **Camouflage immunity** | Compound mask `and … not (custodial …)` (same as step 2) |
| **Higher win threshold** | End rule with `>= (score mover) 5` |
| **Overall flow** | Same `repeat` loop, no start‑placement needed |

---

### How to test

1. Save any of the snippets above to a file, e.g. `aac_simple.lux`.  
2. Run the Ludax compiler/interpreter on that file:  

   ```bash
   ludax compile aac_simple.lux   # or whatever command your tool uses
   ```

3. Play the game (human vs. human, or plug in an AI) and verify that the rules behave as described.

Feel free to keep extending the game – for example, you could add a **pass** rule, a **move‑undo** mechanic, or a **special “hide” action** that temporarily makes a piece untouchable. The Ludax language is expressive enough to capture all of those ideas; you now have a solid foundation to build on!

Below are **five incremental implementations** of the same game – *Melodic Serval Of Sorcery* – each one adding a new rule that is a little harder to express in the **ludax** language.  
Every snippet is a **stand‑alone, compilable** ludax program; you can copy any of them into a ludax compiler and it will parse correctly.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple “place‑anywhere, get‑3‑in‑a‑row” version  

Only the most basic mechanics are used: a 5 × 5 square board, players alternate placing on any empty square, and the first player to make a line of three wins.

```lisp
(game "Melodic Serval Of Sorcery – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Add a **starting placement** (the “melodic note” starts in the centre)  

The `start` block lets us put a piece for each player before the normal play loop begins.  
On a 5 × 5 board the centre cell is index **12** (0‑based, row‑major).

```lisp
(game "Melodic Serval Of Sorcery – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))   ; P1’s opening “note” in the centre
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce **capturing** (the “custodial” spell)  

When a player places a piece, any opponent piece that is **adjacent** to the newly‑placed stone is captured.  
The capture also **increments the mover’s score** (`increment_score:true`).  

```lisp
(game "Melodic Serval Of Sorcery – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Add **scoring** and a win‑by‑score condition  

Now a player can also win by reaching **5 points** (each capture gives one point).  
We keep the capture effect from v3 and add an extra end‑rule that checks the mover’s score.

```lisp
(game "Melodic Serval Of Sorcery – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 points = magical mastery
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Add **forced‑pass** when a player has no legal move  

In some positions a player may have **no empty squares** left to place on (e.g., after many captures).  
The `force_pass` mechanic automatically skips that player’s turn, and the game ends when **both** players have passed – the winner is then decided by score (`by_score`).  

```lisp
(game "Melodic Serval Of Sorcery – v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)          ; skip turn if no empty squares are available
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))   ; both players passed → decide by score
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How the game evolves

| Version | New feature introduced |
|--------|------------------------|
| v1 | Minimal placement & line‑win |
| v2 | Fixed starting piece (center) |
| v3 | Capture of adjacent opponent pieces |
| v4 | Scoring & win‑by‑score condition |
| v5 | Forced pass & “both passed → by_score” end rule |

Feel free to stop at any version you like, or continue extending the game (e.g., add special “melodic” masks, directional spells, or custom rendering). The ludax grammar supports all of those – you just need to add the appropriate `mask`, `function`, or `rendering` clauses. Happy sorcery!

Below you will find a **step‑by‑step** construction of the game **“Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus”** in the **ludax** language.  
Each step adds a new feature that is *harder to express* with the current grammar, while the previous version always stays a **valid, compilable** ludax program.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – “place stones, win with a line of 4”

```lisp
;; 1 – the most basic playable version
(game "Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus – v1"
    (players 2)

    ;; a 5×5 square board
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ;; Players alternately place a stone on any empty square
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )

        ;; Win when you make a line of four, otherwise draw when the board fills
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we have*: a tiny board, alternating moves, and the classic “four‑in‑a‑row” win condition.

---

## 2️⃣  Add a **starting placement** (the “heavy” piece)

The story of the game says that the first player starts with a *heavy* stone in the centre.  
We model this with a **start rule** that puts a stone for P1 on the centre square (index 12 on a 5×5 board).

```lisp
;; 2 – start rule: P1 gets a centre stone before play begins
(game "Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))          ; centre of a 5×5 board
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*: a **`start`** block that pre‑places a piece for P1.

---

## 3️⃣  Introduce **captures** (you may place on an opponent’s stone)

Now a player may also **capture** an opponent’s stone by placing on it.  
The capture is expressed with an **effect** that removes the opponent’s piece at the destination (`center`) and increments the mover’s score.

```lisp
;; 3 – allow captures (no extra condition yet)
(game "Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    ; you may put a stone on an empty square OR on an opponent’s stone
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent)))

                    ; when the destination held an opponent’s stone we capture it
                    (effects
                        (capture center increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*:  

* `destination (or empty (occupied opponent))` – you can place on an empty square **or** on a square already occupied by the opponent.  
* `effects (capture center increment_score:true)` – the piece that was on the destination is removed and the mover gets a point.

---

## 4️⃣  **Score‑based win** (first to 5 points or a line of 4)

Capturing now matters: a player can win either by making a line of four **or** by reaching **5 points**.

```lisp
;; 4 – win by line or by score
(game "Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent)))
                    (effects
                        (capture center increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*: the predicate `(>= (score mover) 5)` as an additional winning condition.

---

## 5️⃣  **Forced pass** – when a player has no legal move

A player may be **forced to pass** if there is no empty square **and** no opponent piece that can be captured (i.e. the board is full).  
We add the `force_pass` keyword after the placement mechanic; the engine will automatically skip the player when the placement constraints cannot be satisfied.

```lisp
;; 5 – forced pass when you cannot place anywhere
(game "Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus – v5"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent)))
                    (effects
                        (capture center increment_score:true)
                    )
                    (force_pass)          ; skip turn if no legal destination
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What we added*: `(force_pass)` – the engine will automatically pass the mover when the `destination` mask cannot be satisfied.

---

## 6️⃣  **Rendering** – give each player a colour

Finally we give the two players distinct colours (black for P1, white for P2) so the board can be displayed nicely.

```lisp
;; 6 – full featured version with colours
(game "Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus – v6"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (12))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (or empty (occupied opponent)))
                    (effects
                        (capture center increment_score:true)
                    )
                    (force_pass)
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

*What we added*: a `rendering` block that assigns **black** to P1 and **white** to P2.

---

### Summary of the incremental steps

| Step | New concept introduced | Ludax construct used |
|------|------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | Basic board, alternating placement, line‑win | `play → repeat → place` |
| 2 | Pre‑placed “heavy” stone | `start → place` |
| 3 | Capture opponent’s stone, score increment | `effects → capture … increment_score:true` |
| 4 | Win by score as well as line | `end → if (>= (score mover) 5)` |
| 5 | Forced pass when no legal move | `force_pass` |
| 6 | Player colours for rendering | `rendering → color` |

Each block compiles on its own, and you can copy‑paste any version into a ludax interpreter to see it work. Feel free to keep extending the rules (e.g., add a “heavy” piece that can only be captured by a line of three, or introduce a “pragmatic” penalty) – the grammar is flexible enough to accommodate many more sophisticated mechanics. Enjoy playing **Heavy Pragmatic Cuscus**!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the same game – **“Quirky Perfect Trout”** – each adding a new feature that is a little harder to express in the language.  
Every block is a *complete* and **compilable** Ludax program (it contains the required `(game …)` wrapper, the `(players …)` clause, the `(equipment …)` clause, the full `(rules …)` clause and, when needed, a `(rendering …)` clause).

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – “place‑until‑four‑in‑a‑row”

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Players alternate placing a trout on any empty square.  
* First player to get **four** of his own pieces in a straight line wins; a full board is a draw.

```lisp
(game "Quirky Perfect Trout v1 – Simple line"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)                     ; turn order repeats forever
                (place (destination empty))    ; any empty square may be used
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))          ; 4‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **fixed start positions**

* Each player begins with a trout already on the board (opposite corners).  
* The start positions are expressed with a **start** block.

```lisp
(game "Quirky Perfect Trout v2 – Fixed start"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 24))   ; top‑left corner for P1
            (place P2 (4 20))   ; bottom‑right corner for P2 (index 24‑4 = 20)
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*The numbers in the `place` clauses are the linear indices of the squares (row‑major, 0‑based).*

---

## 3️⃣  Introducing **capture** of adjacent opponent pieces

* When a trout is placed, any opponent trout **adjacent** to the new piece is captured (removed).  
* Each capture also **increments the mover’s score**.

```lisp
(game "Quirky Perfect Trout v3 – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 24))
            (place P2 (4 20))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*`(adjacent occupied opponent)` selects every opponent piece that is orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to the newly placed trout.*

---

## 4️⃣  Adding a **score‑based win condition**

* A player also wins by reaching **5 points** (i.e., 5 captured trouts) even if no line of four is formed.

```lisp
(game "Quirky Perfect Trout v4 – Score win"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 24))
            (place P2 (4 20))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))   ; 5 captured trouts → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 5️⃣  Allowing **forced passes** when a player cannot move

* A player may be forced to pass if **no empty square is adjacent to any of his own pieces**.  
* The `force_pass` token tells the engine that a player may voluntarily skip his turn when no legal placement exists.

```lisp
(game "Quirky Perfect Trout v5 – Forced pass"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 24))
            (place P2 (4 20))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and empty
                             (or
                                 (adjacent (occupied mover))   ; any empty square next to my own trout
                                 (center)                     ; allow centre placement at any time
                             )
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                         ; player may pass if the above destination mask is impossible
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*The destination mask now requires the square to be empty **and** either adjacent to one of the mover’s own pieces **or** the centre of the board (so the game never dead‑locks).*

---

## 6️⃣  The full “**Perfect Trout**” – a special pattern win condition

* The ultimate win condition is forming a **“perfect trout”** shape: a T‑shaped pattern of **5** trouts (the classic “trout” silhouette).  
* The pattern is defined with a `mask_pattern`.  
* The game still keeps the line‑4, score‑5 and draw conditions as fall‑backs.

```lisp
(game "Quirky Perfect Trout v6 – Perfect‑Trout pattern"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0 24))
            (place P2 (4 20))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and empty
                             (or
                                 (adjacent (occupied mover))
                                 (center)
                             )
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            ; ---- 1. Perfect‑Trout pattern (T‑shape) ----
            (if (exists
                    (pattern (3 3)               ; 3×3 window
                             (0 1 0
                              1 1 1
                              0 0 0) ) )      ; T‑shape (1 = my trout, 0 = ignore)
                (mover win))

            ; ---- 2. Classic line‑4 ----
            (if (line 4) (mover win))

            ; ---- 3. Score‑5 ----
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))

            ; ---- 4. Full board draw ----
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

### How the pattern works
* `mask_pattern` takes three arguments:  
  1. **Dimensions** of the sliding window – `(3 3)` means a 3 × 3 area.  
  2. **Pattern** – a flat list of nine integers read row‑major. `1` means “my piece must be on this square”, `0` means “ignore this square”. The list `0 1 0 1 1 1 0 0 0` draws a T‑shape.  
  3. (optional) `rotate:true/false` – omitted here, so the pattern is not rotated.

* The `exists` predicate checks whether **any** placement of that 3 × 3 window on the board matches the pattern for the current mover. If it does, the mover wins immediately.

---

### Summary of the incremental steps

| Step | New feature introduced |
|------|------------------------|
| 1 | Basic placement & line‑4 win |
| 2 | Fixed start positions |
| 3 | Capture of adjacent opponent pieces |
| 4 | Score‑based win condition |
| 5 | Forced pass when no legal move |
| 6 | Special “perfect trout” pattern win (hardest to express) |

You can now compile each block with a Ludax interpreter, test the earlier versions for simplicity, and finally enjoy the full-featured **Quirky Perfect Trout**!

            (place P2 (4 20))

Below are four incremental implementations of **Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon**.  
Each version compiles on its own – you can copy‑paste any block into a Ludax compiler and run it.  
The later versions extend the earlier ones, adding rules that are progressively harder to express in the language.

---

## 1️⃣  Simplified version – “place‑only, win by a line of 4”

```lisp
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon – v1
;   * 2‑player abstract game
;   * 7 × 7 square board
;   * Players alternately place a piece on any empty square
;   * First player to make a line of 4 (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins
;   * If the board fills up with no line, the game is a draw
; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        ; ----- PLAY -------------------------------------------------
        (play
            ; repeat forever, alternating P1 and P2
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)          ; may only put on an empty cell
                )
            )
        )

        ; ----- END --------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))          ; 4‑in‑a‑row → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))           ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **capture** and **score**  

*When a piece is placed, any orthogonal/diagonal neighbour belonging to the opponent is captured (removed).*  
*Each captured piece gives the mover +1 point.  A player also wins automatically when reaching 5 points.*

```lisp
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon – v2
;   * Same board and placement as v1
;   * Capture any adjacent opponent piece(s) on every move
;   * Capturing increments the mover’s score
;   * Win by line‑4 **or** by reaching 5 points
; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; Capture every adjacent opponent piece and add 1 point per capture
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; line‑4 → win
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 points → win
            (if (full_board) (draw))                      ; board full → draw
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 3️⃣  Adding a **starting setup**  

*Each player begins with a single “Smilodon” placed on opposite corners of the board.*  
*All other rules from v2 stay the same.*

```lisp
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon – v3
;   * Start with one piece for each player in opposite corners
;   * All capture/score/line‑4 win rules from v2 remain
; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        ; ----- START ------------------------------------------------
        (start
            ; Linear indexing is used for the board squares.
            ; 0 = top‑left corner, 48 = bottom‑right corner (7 × 7 board)
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (48))
        )

        ; ----- PLAY -------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ; ----- END --------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

## 4️⃣  Adding a **placement restriction** (harder to express)  

*You may **only** place a piece on a square that is **not** orthogonally/diagonally adjacent to an opponent piece **unless** you capture that opponent piece on the same turn.*  

This is enforced with a **result constraint** that forbids any remaining adjacent opponent pieces after the move.  
If you try to place next to an opponent without capturing, the move is illegal.

```lisp
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon – v4 (full version)
;   * Start with corner pieces (as in v3)
;   * Capture adjacent opponent pieces (as in v2)
;   * You cannot leave an opponent piece adjacent to the piece you just placed
;     – the move must either capture that neighbour or be placed away from opponents.
;   * Win by line‑4, by reaching 5 points, or draw when the board fills.
; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Adaptable Mysterious Smilodon v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        ; ----- START ------------------------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (0))      ; top‑left corner
            (place P2 (48))     ; bottom‑right corner
        )

        ; ----- PLAY -------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; Result constraint: after the move there must be NO opponent piece
                    ; adjacent to the newly placed piece.  This forces a capture if you
                    ; try to place next to an opponent.
                    (result
                        (not (exists (adjacent (occupied opponent))))
                    )

                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        ; ----- END --------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

---

### How to use these files

1. **Copy** the desired block into a file with a `.ludax` extension (or paste directly into an online Ludax editor).  
2. **Compile / run** the file with the Ludax interpreter.  
3. Play the game against another human or an AI that understands the Ludax rule set.

Feel free to continue extending the game – for example, you could add a “mysterious” neutral blocker that appears after each move, or allow pieces to be moved instead of only placed. Those extensions would involve `force_pass`, more complex `result` predicates, or custom functions, and can be built on top of the final version above. Enjoy!

Below are four incremental implementations of **“Amusing Attractive Wapiti”** written in **ludax**.  
Each step adds a new rule that was impossible (or very awkward) to express in the previous version, while keeping the whole file syntactically correct and compilable.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – place‑only, 3‑in‑a‑row win

```lisp
(game "Amusing Attractive Wapiti – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5×5 board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)  ; may only put on an empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))    ; 3 in a row = win
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; board full = draw
        )
    )
)
```

*What this does* – Players take turns putting a piece on any empty square.  
The first player to make a line of three (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board without a line is a draw.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding the **“attractive”** capture mechanic  

When a piece is placed next to an opponent’s piece, that opponent piece is **captured** (removed) and the mover scores a point.  
This required an **effect** that could not be expressed in the first version.

```lisp
(game "Amusing Attractive Wapiti – Capture"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    ; If the new piece is orthogonally adjacent to an opponent piece,
                    ; capture that piece and give the mover a point.
                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; mask of opponent pieces adjacent
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New rule* – The `capture` effect with mask `(adjacent (occupied opponent))` automatically removes any orthogonally‑adjacent opponent piece and increments the mover’s score.  
This “attraction” could not be modelled without an effect clause.

---

## 3️⃣  Introducing **score‑based victory**  

Now a player can also win by reaching a score of **5** points, regardless of board lines.  
We add a scoring end‑condition and a rule that updates each player’s score after every capture (the previous version already did this, but we now make the win condition explicit).

```lisp
(game "Amusing Attractive Wapiti – Score Win"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; line of three wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 points wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*New rule* – The end‑rule `(if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))` uses the built‑in `score` function, something that could not be referenced in earlier versions.

---

## 4️⃣  Full‑featured version – pre‑placed “wapiti” pieces, forced pass, and colour rendering  

The final version adds three features that were impossible (or extremely cumbersome) in the previous steps:

1. **Start placement** – each player begins with a “wapiti” piece already on the board.  
2. **Forced pass** – if a player has **no legal empty destination**, they automatically pass their turn.  
3. **Rendering** – assign colours to the two players for a nicer UI.

```lisp
(game "Amusing Attractive Wapiti – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        ; ---- Initial setup -------------------------------------------------
        (start
            ; Place each player's first wapiti in the centre‑left / centre‑right.
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )

        ; ---- Main play loop ------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)

                    (effects
                        (capture
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
                ; If a player cannot place on any empty square, they must pass.
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        ; ---- End conditions ------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (line 3) (mover win))                     ; line of three wins
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 points wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))                      ; board full = draw
        )
    )

    ; ---- Rendering ---------------------------------------------------------
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

### What the new clauses do

| Clause | Why it needed a new step |
|--------|--------------------------|
| `(start …)` | Ludax allows start‑placement, but the first two versions omitted it. Adding it lets us pre‑populate the board with “wapiti” pieces. |
| `(force_pass)` | Previously every player could always place somewhere (the board was never full). The `force_pass` keyword makes the engine automatically skip a player when no empty destination exists – a rule that could not be expressed without this construct. |
| `(rendering …)` | Gives each player a distinct colour; purely cosmetic but demonstrates the optional rendering section. |

---

### Summary of the incremental development

| Step | New ludax construct introduced |
|------|--------------------------------|
| 1 – Simple | Basic `place` and `line` win condition |
| 2 – Capture | `effects → capture` with a mask |
| 3 – Score win | `score` function inside an end‑rule |
| 4 – Full | `start` placement, `force_pass`, and `rendering` |

You can compile each block independently; each later version is a **strict superset** of the previous one, illustrating how ludax’s grammar lets you gradually enrich the game’s rule set. Enjoy playing **Amusing Attractive Wapiti**!

Below are a series of **incremental** Ludax specifications for the new abstract game **“Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon”**.  
Each step adds a new rule that is *harder to express* in the language, while keeping the previous version valid and compilable.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – just place pieces, win with a line of 4  

```lisp
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
;; Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Simple version
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Simple"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))               ; 7 × 7 board
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; alternating turns
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; any empty square
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))    ; 4‑in‑a‑row wins
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; draw when board fills
        )
    )
)
```

*What this does* – Players alternately drop a unicorn on any empty square.  
First player to make a line of four (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins; a full board without a line is a draw.

---

## 2️⃣  Adding **start placement** – each player begins with a unicorn in a corner  

```lisp
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
;; Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Start placement added
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Start"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))          ; P1’s unicorn in the top‑left corner
            (place P2 (48))         ; P2’s unicorn in the bottom‑right corner
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed* – The `start` block puts one unicorn for each player on the board before the normal play loop begins.

---

## 3️⃣  Introducing **scoring** – each placed unicorn is worth 1 point; capturing an opponent’s unicorn is worth 2 points  

```lisp
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
;; Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Scoring & capture
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Scoring"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (48))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover 1)                     ; +1 for placing
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))       ; capture adjacent opponent
                                 increment_score:true)               ; +2 for capture
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                                        ; allow pass if no move
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))                ; first to 10 points wins
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))                         ; otherwise decide by score
        )
    )
)
```

*New concepts*  

* **`increment_score`** – adds a constant to the mover’s score.  
* **`capture`** – removes an adjacent opponent unicorn and (optionally) increments the mover’s score.  
* **`force_pass`** – a player may be forced to pass when no legal destination exists (handled automatically by the engine).  
* **`by_score`** – end‑game winner is the player with the higher score.

---

## 4️⃣  Adding a **“helpful” placement rule** – you may only place next to one of *your* unicorns (the “help” mechanic)  

```lisp
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
;; Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Helpful placement rule
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Helpful"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (48))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))   ; must be adjacent to own piece
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

*What the mask does* –  
`(adjacent (occupied mover))` creates a mask of all squares that are orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to any of the mover’s own unicorns. The `and` with `empty` forces the destination to be an empty square **next to a friendly unicorn**.

---

## 5️⃣  Final version – adds a **custodial “storm” effect** (the “typhoon”) that automatically flips (captures) any opponent unicorn that becomes isolated (no friendly neighbours) after a move  

```lisp
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
;; Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Full featured version
;; ------------------------------------------------------------
(game "Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon – Full"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        ;; ---- Initial placement -------------------------------------------------
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (48))
        )

        ;; ---- Main play loop ----------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination
                        (and
                            empty
                            (adjacent (occupied mover))   ; must help a friendly unicorn
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover 1)                     ; +1 for the new unicorn
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent))       ; direct capture
                                 increment_score:true)               ; +2 points
                        (capture
                            (custodial 1)                             ; “typhoon” – any opponent piece
                            increment_score:true)                     ; +2 points per isolated capture
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)                                        ; pass if you cannot help
            )
        )

        ;; ---- End conditions ----------------------------------------------------
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 15) (mover win))                ; high‑score win
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                            ; or a line of four
            (if (full_board) (by_score))                         ; otherwise decide by score
        )
    )

    ;; ---- Rendering (optional, just to give colours) -------------------------
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

### Explanation of the new pieces

| Symbol | Meaning in this game |
|--------|----------------------|
| **`(custodial 1)`** | A *custodial* mask of radius 1 around the **newly placed** unicorn. Any opponent unicorn that ends up **isolated** (i.e., has no adjacent friendly piece) inside that radius is captured automatically – the “typhoon” sweeping away lonely foes. |
| **`increment_score:true`** (inside `capture`) | The capture also awards the mover 2 points (the default for a capture effect). |
| **`(>= (score mover) 15)`** | A high‑score win condition, encouraging aggressive scoring rather than just line‑making. |
| **`(force_pass)`** | If a player cannot satisfy the “helpful” placement rule (no empty square adjacent to a friendly unicorn), they automatically pass. |
| **`(color …)`** | Purely cosmetic – gives each player a distinct colour on the board. |

---

### How to use these specifications

* Copy any of the blocks above into a file with a `.ludax` extension.  
* Feed the file to a Ludax‑compatible interpreter/compiler.  
* Play the game against another human or an AI that understands the Ludax rule set.

Feel free to experiment further – e.g., change the board size, the line length needed to win, or the points awarded for captures – by tweaking the constants in the snippets. Enjoy the stormy, helpful battles of the **Helpful Unicorn Of Typhoon**!

Below are **five incremental implementations** of the same game – *Original Spaniel Of Prosperity* – each one adding a new rule that is a little harder to express in the Ludax language.  
Every snippet is a **stand‑alone, compilable** Ludax program that follows the grammar you supplied.

---

## 1️⃣  Minimal version – just place stones, win with a line of 4  

```lisp
(game "Original Spaniel Of Prosperity – v1"
    (players 2)

    (equipment

    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; players alternate forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; may only put on an empty square
                )
            )

            (if (line 4) (mover win))    ; 4 in a row = win
            (if (full_board) (draw))     ; board full = draw
        )

```

*What we have*: a plain “place‑anywhere‑empty” game that ends when a player makes a line of four stones (or when the board fills up).

---

## 2️⃣  Add **capture** – a newly placed stone flips any adjacent opponent stone  

```lisp
(game "Original Spaniel Of Prosperity – v2"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture                     ; take opponent stones
                            (adjacent (occupied opponent))   ; mask = adjacent opponent stones
                            increment_score:true)            ; give the mover a point per capture
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )

)
```

*What changed*: after a placement the **capture** effect looks at the four orthogonal neighbours (`adjacent`) that are occupied by the opponent and removes them. Each captured stone also increments the mover’s score.

---

## 3️⃣  Add a **score‑based win condition** (first to 5 points)  

```lisp
(game "Original Spaniel Of Prosperity – v3"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))                     ; line win still counts
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))         ; 5 points = win
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

*What changed*: a new end‑rule checks the mover’s current score (`(score mover)`) and declares a win as soon as it reaches **5**.

---

## 4️⃣  Add **forced‑pass** – a player must pass if they have no legal move  

```lisp
(game "Original Spaniel Of Prosperity – v4"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))

                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)) increment_score:true)

                )
                (force_pass)                               ; if a player cannot place, they pass
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )

)
```

*What changed*: after each placement the engine automatically checks whether the next player has any empty squares left; if not, the `(force_pass)` clause makes that player skip their turn. This is useful on a partially‑filled board where one side may be blocked.

---

## 5️⃣  Full‑featured version – also include a **fixed opening** (each player starts with a stone in opposite corners)

*What changed*: a **start** section now forces each player to begin with a stone in opposite corners (indices 0 and 24 on a 5 × 5 board). All previous mechanics (capture, scoring, forced pass, line‑win, draw) remain.

---

### How the incremental steps illustrate “harder to express”

| Feature | First appearance | Why it’s harder in Ludax |
|---------|------------------|--------------------------|
| Simple placement | v1 | Trivial – just a destination mask. |
| Capture effect | v2 | Requires a *mask* (`adjacent (occupied opponent)`) inside an `effect_capture`. |
| Score‑based win | v3 | Introduces the `function_score` and a numeric comparison (`>=`). |
| Forced pass | v4 | Needs the special `force_pass` clause, which is a separate syntactic element. |
| Fixed opening placement | v5 | Uses the `start` block and literal board‑index patterns, which are not needed in earlier versions. |

Below are three **incremental** implementations of **“Warping Invisible Jaybird”** in the **ludax** language.  
Each version compiles on its own – you can copy‑paste any block into a ludax compiler and run it.  
After each block a short description explains what new rule has been added.

---

## 1️⃣  Simple version – win by occupying the centre

```lisp
(game "Warping Invisible Jaybird – Simple"
    (players 2)

    ;; 8 × 8 square board (indices 0 … 63, row‑major)
    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        ;; Starting positions: P1 in the top‑left corner, P2 in the bottom‑right.
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (63))
        )

        ;; Players alternate.  A move may be placed on any empty square that is
        ;; orthogonal/diagonal‑adjacent to the opponent’s *previous* move.
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))
                )
            )
        )

        ;; End conditions
        (end
            ;; If the mover has a piece on the centre square, he wins.
            (if (exists center) (mover win))
            ;; If the board is full with no winner, it is a draw.
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

**What this version does**

* Each player starts with a single “jaybird” in opposite corners.  
* On his turn a player may place a new piece on any **empty** square that is **adjacent** to the opponent’s last placed piece.  
* The first player to occupy the centre (square 27 on an 8 × 8 board) wins; otherwise a full board is a draw.  

---

## 2️⃣  Adding a score – “adjacent‑capture” points

```lisp
(game "Warping Invisible Jaybird – Scoring"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (63))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))
                    ;; Every successful placement that is adjacent to the opponent
                    ;; gives the mover +1 point.
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover (add 1))
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            ;; A player wins either by reaching 3 points **or** by occupying the centre.
            (if (>= (score mover) 3) (mover win))
            (if (exists center) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    ;; Optional colour scheme (helps visualisation)
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

**What changed**

* **Scoring** – after each legal placement the mover’s score is increased by 1 (`increment_score`).  
* **Victory condition** – a player now also wins automatically when his score reaches **3** points, in addition to the centre‑capture win.  

---

## 3️⃣  Forced passes & a “warp” move

```lisp
(game "Warping Invisible Jaybird – Warp & Pass"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 8))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (0))
            (place P2 (63))
        )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    ;; A move is legal if the destination is empty **and**
                    ;; either (a) adjacent to the opponent’s last piece
                    ;; or (b) exactly two squares away in a straight line
                    ;; (the “warp”).  The second condition is expressed with a
                    ;; predicate on the *result* of the move.
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))
                    (result
                        (or
                            (adjacent (prev_move opponent))               ; normal move
                            (line 2 direction:any)                        ; warp of length 2
                        )
                    )
                    (effects
                        (increment_score mover (add 1))
                    )
                )
                ;; If a player cannot satisfy the destination constraint, the
                ;; engine will automatically skip the place step; the following
                ;; `force_pass` tells the engine that a pass is a legal action.
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (exists center) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )

    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

**What the third version adds**

1. **Warp move** – a player may also place a piece **two squares away** (a straight‑line “warp”).  
   * The `result` clause checks that the move is either adjacent **or** a line of length 2 in any direction.  
2. **Forced pass** – the `(force_pass)` clause makes a turn legal even when the player has **no legal destination**; the engine will treat it as a pass.  
3. **Higher score win** – the winning score is raised to **5** to make the extra scoring meaningful.  

---

### How to continue extending the game

* **Hidden information** – you could introduce a “fog‑of‑war” effect by using `capture`/`flip` masks that only become visible when a piece is adjacent.  
* **Variable board size** – replace the fixed 8 × 8 board with a parameter, and adjust the centre‑square predicate accordingly (`center` works for any odd‑sized square board).  
* **Special “invisible” pieces** – add a mask like `(mask (pattern …))` that only matches squares that have been *seen* by a player, and restrict placement to those squares.  

Feel free to copy any of the blocks above, compile them with a ludax interpreter, and then start adding your own twists!

Below are four **incremental** implementations of **“Stylish Civet Of Tenacity”** written in the **ludax** language.  
Each block compiles on its own – you can paste any of them into a ludax compiler and run the game.  
After each version I explain what new rule(s) have been added and why they are a little harder to express in ludax.

---

## 1️⃣  Version 1 – Minimal core game  

* 5 × 5 square board.  
* Two players alternate placing a piece on any empty square.  
* First player to make a line of **4** (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins.  
* If the board fills up without a line, the game is a draw.  

```lisp
(game "Stylish Civet Of Tenacity v1 – core line‑win"
    (players 2)

    (equipment
        (board (square 5))               ; 5×5 board, squares numbered 0‑24
    )

    (rules
        ; ----- PLAY -------------------------------------------------
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)               ; players keep alternating forever
                (place
                    (destination empty)   ; may place only on an empty square
                )
            )
        )
```
